Drinking a decoction of henna bark and leaves helps prevent kidney stones and treats urinary disorders. 7. Women use henna paste on their hands as a decorative dye; it has only cosmetic purposes and provides a cooling effect to the body. 8. One part henna juice mixed with three parts sesame oil or coconut oil, heated together and applied on the head at night, helps promote hair health and provide relief from headaches. 9. Applying henna flower paste on the forehead helps treat insomnia and provides calming, sedative effects for those who have difficulty sleeping. 10. For urinary tract infections, applying henna flower paste on the lower abdomen helps reduce burning sensation during urination.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf juice: 7-14 mL with 50-100 mL water, 3 times daily for jaundice. Leaf paste: 2 spoonfuls in buttermilk for stomach issues. Leaf decoction with milk and sugar for 24 days as a body tonic. Oil preparation: 1 part juice to 3 parts oil.

medicinal_uses: 1. Rubbing sandalwood on a wet stone and consuming the paste (approximately one tola of sandalwood paste with a tola of sugar) mixed with water, once or twice a week for 1-2 weeks, helps treat urinary burning and infections. 2. Rubbing sandalwood and mixing with buttermilk, applying the paste on the forehead, helps cure headaches. 3. Rubbing sandalwood with sesame oil and applying on boils and pustules on the skin helps reduce them; applying sandalwood paste on skin conditions where there are nodules also helps. 4. Rubbing sandalwood horn with milk and mixing with sandalwood oil, applying on the body for several days, helps treat skin conditions. Sandalwood oil and soap with sandalwood essence help keep the body fragrant. 5. Mixing sandalwood horn in milk and applying helps treat dryness, cough, and respiratory disorders. 6. Applying sandalwood oil (2-3 drops) on the ears helps prevent ear discharge and infection.
dosage_preparation: Sandalwood paste: approximately 1 tola with sugar, 1-2 times per week. Sandalwood oil: 2-3 drops for ear application. External paste with milk or buttermilk for skin and headache. Veterinary use: Sandalwood oil mixed with camphor applied externally on cattle wounds and skin lesions.

latin_name: Erythroxylum monogynum Roxb. (= E. indicum (DC.) Bedd.)
medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming the root with chitramula (Plumbago) root and sandalwood helps treat related urogenital diseases and disorders. 2. Consuming the bark decoction helps treat abdominal pain, fever, urinary tract problems, and related conditions. 3. Using the bark decoction with dried ginger, rock salt, asafoetida, and milk helps treat joint pain and rheumatic conditions. 4. The oil extracted from the plant is used externally to treat itching, skin rashes, and other dermatological conditions. 5. Consuming leaf juice with sugar and eating it with honey helps enhance physical strength and improves male vitality.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction with dried ginger, rock salt, and asafoetida for rheumatic conditions. Leaf juice with sugar for vitality enhancement. Oil for external skin application.

medicinal_uses: 1. Drinking the juice of marigold flower petals helps purify the blood. 2. Consuming the flower petal juice also helps treat blood-related disorders and related conditions. 3. Grinding flower petals, adding sugar and mixing with sesame oil, placing in a clay vessel and burying underground for a month, then extracting the mixture, straining and applying the oil on areas with baldness helps promote hair regrowth and darkening of hair when stored in a bottle and applied from the bottle to the scalp regularly.
dosage_preparation: Flower petal juice for blood purification. Oil preparation: flower petals ground with sugar and sesame oil, placed in clay pot underground for 1 month, then filtered and stored for topical application on bald areas.

latin_name: Solanum erianthum D. Don (= S. verbascifolium L.)
medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding the dried root of the plant, soaking in hot water, and applying the paste as a poultice helps reduce swelling and provide pain relief. 2. During pregnancy, when there is excess bleeding, grinding the fresh root juice, straining, and mixing with a little ghee helps provide strength and stops the bleeding. 3. Grinding the fresh root with turmeric and wax, mixing together, and applying on areas with body pain helps relieve the affected areas. 4. For piles and hemorrhoids, grinding the fresh plant leaves and applying the paste externally helps reduce the swelling and associated discomfort.
dosage_preparation: Root paste soaked in hot water for poultice application. Fresh root juice with ghee for pregnancy bleeding. Leaf paste for hemorrhoid application.

latin_name: Shorea roxburghii G. Don (=S. talura Roxb.)
medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming the gum (resin) of the tree with honey or sugar helps cure dysentery and diarrhea.
dosage_preparation: Mix tree gum with honey or sugar and consume orally.

medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding the root with turmeric and applying on the body relieves body odor and skin irritation. 2. Consuming the decoction of the root helps relieve constipation, heart-related issues, and cough.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction taken internally; root paste with turmeric applied externally.

medicinal_uses: 1. Eating the bark of Jambuneerale dried and powdered helps stop nausea and vomiting. 2. Bark decoction used as a gargle helps cure mouth ulcers and strengthens teeth and gums. 3. Applying powdered bark on insect/scorpion stings provides quick relief. 4. Bark decoction consumed by women helps treat uterine disorders and excessive menstrual bleeding; however, pregnant women should avoid this remedy. 5. A paste made from grinding one part leaves with five parts sesame oil, when applied, treats boils and ulcers on the legs. 6. In moderate quantities, consuming juice prepared from jamun fruit, mango, and bilva tree leaves mixed with about 7-14 ml of juice on an empty stomach 3 times per day helps improve blood count (treats anemia). 7. Bark juice (7-14 ml) mixed with sugar, consumed 3 times daily, helps treat excessive acid reflux in the mouth. 8. Eating jamun fruit helps relieve urinary-related conditions. 9. Bark decoction used as a gargle addresses dental and oral health problems. 10. Bark powder consumed with buttermilk addresses stomach and digestive issues. 11. Seed powder consumed with water is beneficial for diabetic patients.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction: 7-14 ml taken 3 times daily. Bark powder mixed with water or buttermilk. Veterinary use: Boil bark pieces with jaggery and water, give to cattle morning and night for 2-3 days to treat bloody diarrhea.

medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming the fruit decoction with cumin, pepper, and jaggery (about 10 ml each) helps treat cough, cold, and related respiratory disorders. 2. The kernel, pepper, dried ginger, and similar herbs combined make an effective remedy for eye diseases, headaches, and dizziness. 3. Consuming ripe fruit paste (about 10 ml) mixed with honey helps treat urinary retention and constipation. 4. Mixing one part fruit powder, one part honey, one part ghee with one spoon of butter, consumed regularly, promotes healthy skin and prevents aging. 5. Grinding the fruit and applying on swollen or ulcerated areas on hands and legs helps the infection to subside. 6. Sprinkling fruit powder on wounds helps stop bleeding. 7. Consuming the fruit pulp helps treat constipation. 8. Pressing the fruit and applying on areas of skin disease helps cure the condition. 9. Fruit powder, consumed daily, helps reduce impotence and promotes reproductive health. 10. Oil prepared from the seed, when applied on the body, reduces body odor.
dosage_preparation: Fruit decoction: approximately 10 ml. Fruit powder mixed with honey and ghee for oral consumption. Oil from seeds for external application.

medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming lotus flower and rhizome decoction helps cure piles, bleeding disorders, and related conditions. 2. Lotus stem and rhizome consumed in food helps treat digestive issues and improves appetite. 3. Lotus seeds ground into powder and consumed with milk helps treat leucorrhea and urinary disorders. 4. Lotus petal juice mixed with sugar helps cure excessive thirst and bilious conditions. 5. Juice prepared from lotus leaves mixed with various herbs, when consumed by those affected with poisoning, acts as an antidote. 6. Applying crushed lotus leaves on the forehead helps alleviate fever and headache. 7. Powder from dried lotus petals or flower buds, when consumed with honey, strengthens the brain and body and heals wounds and ulcers. 8. Sherbet prepared from lotus rhizomes helps treat burning sensation during urination and skin diseases. 9. Grinding the rhizome petals and applying on the face helps reduce dark spots and blemishes. 10. Lotus flower decoction (50-100 ml) consumed daily for several days helps boost memory and cognitive ability in children. 11. Lotus petal and stamen juice (about 4 spoons) mixed with half a spoon of licorice, consumed daily for 7-14 days, helps treat anemia (rakta moolavyadhi). 12. Eating lotus flower stamens with fresh cow butter helps treat bleeding disorders (hemorrhagic conditions). 13. A paste made from lotus flower petals helps treat heart disease. 14. Lotus seed powder applied on the body helps strengthen and nourish the skin and promote beauty.
dosage_preparation: Lotus flower decoction: 50-100 ml. Petal and stamen juice: 4 teaspoons. Lotus rhizome preparations taken 2-3 times daily. Panchakarma recipe: Mix lotus rhizome 1 part, dried ginger root 2 parts, pepper 2 parts, cardamom 2 parts, cinnamon 2 parts; grind and consume with honey morning and evening for 2-3 days for joint pain relief.

medicinal_uses: 1. The bark of Tigadu tree, combined with Parijata and Trishala, is boiled and the decoction consumed to act as a remedy for snake poisoning and toxic bites. 2. Root bark decoction, taken daily for 15 days (about three pieces of root bark boiled in one portion of water, reduced to one-third), helps treat arthritis, blood disorders, and stomach cramps. 3. A poultice made from the bark and fruit is applied on boils and swellings on the body. 4. Bark and coconut oil, boiled together to prepare a medicated oil, when applied helps treat itching and skin infections. 5. Young leaves cooked as a vegetable help relieve gas and bloating. 6. Consuming the fruit or root paste for a few days helps prevent diarrhea. 7. The seed decoction consumed like warm tea helps with tissue repair.
dosage_preparation: Root bark decoction: Boil 3 pieces in water, reduce to one-third, consume daily for 15 days.

medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding the root with Tulasi leaves and applying on the body helps relieve stinging nettle rashes and body odor. 2. Consuming the root decoction helps cure bloating and flatulence. 3. Grinding the root and consuming it helps treat filariasis (Filarialis) - it relieves the swelling in limbs and legs caused by the parasitic infection. It also helps alleviate fever related to the condition.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction for internal consumption. Root paste with Tulasi leaves for external application.

medicinal_uses: 1. Tumbe plant's 2 drops of leaf juice instilled into the nostrils helps relieve headache and cold congestion (it may cause sneezing which helps clear sinuses). 2. Leaf juice mixed with pepper, dried ginger, cumin, and jaggery creates a decoction for respiratory ailments. 3. About 1 spoon of flower juice mixed with 1/4 spoon of honey, consumed daily, helps treat asthma and breathing difficulties (continue for 3 months). 4. Tumbe plant juice (1/4 litre) mixed with 1 measure of rice, 2 cups of castor oil, and 1 measure of sesame oil heated together helps treat rheumatic pain and body aches for 3-4 days. 5. For 21 days, consuming specified quantities of Tumbe juice with other herbs helps treat worm infections: 4-6 drops for children, 1-2 teaspoons for adults, taken with warm water. 6. Mixing Tumbe plant 5 parts with 10 parts water, reducing to 2-3 parts, combined with jaggery (3-4 cups), helps treat severe cough. 7. Mixing specified quantities (2-3 spoons) of Tumbe extract with 5 parts honey and other herbs helps cure chronic cold and flu conditions. 8. About 10-12 flower heads mixed with 10 ml ghee and 2 pinches of turmeric form a remedy for various ailments. 9. Tumbe flower extract when applied on the skin helps treat ringworm and fungal infections. 10. Consuming the indicated preparations (approximately 10 cups) helps treat intestinal parasites. 11. Tumbe plant juice when applied on snake bite areas may help reduce swelling. Veterinary: 4 fistfuls of Tumbe flowers with 1 fistful of salt, applied to cattle wounds, helps heal them. Tumbe flower juice mixed with salt applied to nasal passages (nostrils) helps treat cold symptoms in cattle. Tumbe flower paste mixed with appropriate herbs given to cattle after childbirth helps reduce fever and inflammation. Tumbe leaf juice applied on the body helps treat cattle skin diseases (scabies). In traditional veterinary medicine, various Tumbe preparations are used for digestive and respiratory ailments in livestock.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice: 2 drops nasally. Flower juice with honey: 1 spoon daily for 3 months for asthma. Decoction with herbs: 4-6 drops for children, 1-2 teaspoons for adults. Veterinary: 4 fistfuls of flowers with salt for cattle wounds.

medicinal_uses: 1. Mixing teak bark in goat milk, boiling, and consuming the decoction helps prevent urinary problems and bedwetting. 2. Bark decoction applied on the scalp and body helps relieve itching and pain caused by skin conditions and also helps reduce inflammation. 3. Teak leaf juice heated and applied externally helps treat erysipelas (a bacterial skin infection). 4. Teak seeds ground to a paste and mixed with a small amount of fragrance oil, applied on the forehead and body, helps dissolve kidney stones over several days of treatment. 5. Oil prepared from teak seeds applied on the scalp promotes hair growth and prevents hair loss. 6. Oil from teak seeds applied on the body helps prevent fainting or loss of consciousness.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction in goat milk for urinary complaints. Teak seed oil for topical application on scalp and body. Leaf juice for external application on skin infections.

latin_name: Terminalia arjuna (Roxb. ex DC.) Wt. & Arn.
medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming half a tola of Toremutthi bark powder twice daily (morning and evening with honey) for 2-3 months helps treat heart-related diseases and complications. 2. Applying leaf juice helps treat ringworm and skin conditions. 3. Bark decoction or powder consumed orally helps treat menstrual disorders. 4. Bark powder sprinkled on wounds helps stop bleeding. 5. Consuming the bark helps treat snake and scorpion bites. 6. Grinding the bark and applying on the area under the tongue and gums helps cure mouth-related diseases.
dosage_preparation: Bark powder: 1/2 tola, consumed with honey, twice daily for 2-3 months for heart diseases.

medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding the root and applying on wounds and cuts helps heal injuries faster. 2. Consuming about 1 gram of root powder helps treat intestinal parasites and promotes bowel movement. 3. Applying Datturi plant's yellow milk (latex, about 1/2 ml) or its juice (about 5 ml) externally helps treat itching, eczema, and various skin disorders. 4. Grinding the seeds and applying the paste on areas affected by joint inflammation and pain helps provide relief. 5. Seed oil or plant sap applied on the skin helps treat various dermatological conditions. 6. Seed powder (about 200 mg) divided into four portions, consumed one portion per day along with four fistfuls of butter, helps cure cough and phlegm. This treatment should be continued for several days with a doctor's supervision. 7. Mothers producing excessive breast milk can apply Datturi milk (latex) on the breast area to reduce excess milk production. 8. Datturi plant's milk or seed oil applied on scalp helps treat baldness and promotes hair regrowth when applied regularly. 9. Applying the fresh plant's latex on decayed teeth and painful gums helps reduce toothache and dental pain (also helps with swelling of the jaw).
dosage_preparation: Root powder: 1 gram orally. Latex: 1/2 ml externally. Seed powder: 200 mg divided into 4 daily portions. Veterinary: Datturi seeds 5 grams mixed with jaggery, given to cattle as a bolus, helps treat intestinal conditions over 2-3 days.

medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming the root powder helps act as a surgical tonic. 2. Consuming the root powder helps treat snake bites. 3. Consuming root decoction mixed with black pepper and milk in the morning daily helps treat rheumatic and chronic joint diseases. 4. Leaf and root decoction consumed helps treat joint-related disorders (sandhivata), arthritis, asthma, and digestive ailments. It also helps reduce fever and purify the blood. 5. Dagadi leaves and root paste applied externally treats various dermatological diseases; when applied on cuts and wounds, it helps reduce pain and swelling. 6. Leaf juice consumed with sugar helps treat inflammation, urinary disorders, and provides cooling effect to the body. 7. Leaf juice mixed with water, when used as an eye wash, helps treat eye infections (gonorrheal ophthalmia). 8. Leaf juice mixed with water applied as drops in the ear helps treat ear discharge and prevents infections. 9. Consuming Dagadi plant extract helps improve sexual vigor and reproductive strength and increases semen quality.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction with black pepper and milk daily. Dagadi powder: 3 parts, with triphaladi powder 1 part and ginger powder 1 part, ground in water and consumed for 3 days for veterinary conditions.

medicinal_uses: 1. A combination of cinnamon, bay leaf, cardamom, and other spices ground together and consumed with hot water (about 10 grams in a cup, 3-4 times daily) helps relieve cold, cough, congestion, and flu symptoms. 2. Mixing cinnamon powder with warm water and gargling helps treat throat infections, tonsillitis, and gum problems; this also helps reduce body heat. 3. Chewing cinnamon in the mouth helps maintain oral hygiene and prevent bad breath, tooth decay, and gum diseases. 4. Applying cinnamon paste externally helps treat dengue fever. Consuming cinnamon decoction helps alleviate indigestion, flatulence, fever, diarrhea, excess urination, and related complaints. 5. Consuming cinnamon (dalchini) helps regulate menstrual flow when it is irregular. 6. Eating cinnamon as a food ingredient helps prevent nausea and vomiting. 7. [unclear]. 8. Applying cinnamon oil on the skin helps treat dental pain and tooth sensitivity. 9. Applying cinnamon oil on reproductive organs helps treat erectile dysfunction (loss of erection). 10. Applying cinnamon oil to areas affected by eye-related conditions helps promote healing. 11. Cinnamon oil applied on bitten areas helps repel insects.
dosage_preparation: Spice mix: about 10 grams in hot water, 3-4 times daily. Cinnamon oil for topical application. Cinnamon powder gargle for oral health.

medicinal_uses: 1. Taking 4-6 red hibiscus flowers, removing the stamen, grinding the petals and bottom part together, adding 1 teaspoon of rock sugar, and consuming twice daily helps treat excessive menstrual bleeding (menorrhagia). Continue for up to 12 months (1-2 flowers daily). For older women, consuming 2-3 flowers daily helps regulate menstruation. This treatment is effective for heavy periods, clotted menses, and related uterine disorders.
dosage_preparation: 4-6 red hibiscus flowers processed with rock sugar, consumed twice daily. For long-term treatment, 1-2 flowers daily for up to 12 months.

medicinal_uses: 1. Pomegranate fruit juice, when consumed regularly, helps treat anemia and improves hemoglobin levels. 2. The rind of pomegranate fruit dried and powdered, when consumed, helps treat diarrhea and dysentery. 3. Pomegranate flower buds ground and consumed help treat nasal bleeding (epistaxis). 4. Consuming pomegranate fruit helps improve digestion and appetite. 5. Pomegranate rind decoction used as a gargle helps strengthen gums and cure mouth ulcers. 6. The bark of the root consumed as a decoction helps expel intestinal worms (anthelmintic). 7. Pomegranate juice mixed with other herbs consumed regularly helps strengthen the heart. 8. Consuming pomegranate seed powder with buttermilk helps treat digestive problems and 4-5 grams daily helps maintain gut health. 9. Pomegranate fruit juice consumed with food helps treat morning sickness and nausea during pregnancy. 10. Pomegranate rind boiled in water and used as a wash for wounds helps in healing. 11. Pomegranate bark used for deworming - approximately 100 ml decoction consumed helps expel tapeworms. 12. Pomegranate skin or bark decoction helps treat leucorrhea (white discharge). 13. Applying fresh pomegranate juice on the face helps maintain skin health and glow. 14. Pomegranate root bark decoction (with sugar) helps treat joint pain and rheumatic conditions for 3-4 days.
dosage_preparation: Fruit juice consumed regularly. Rind powder: 4-5 grams daily with buttermilk. Root bark decoction: approximately 100 ml for deworming. Panchakarma recipe: 10 measures of fresh pomegranate rind mixed with various spices (pepper 100g, coriander 100g, cinnamon, cardamom, etc.) ground and consumed 1 measure with honey, twice daily for 2-3 months.

latin_name: Coleus amboinicus Lour. (=C. aromaticus Benth. = Plectranthus amboinicus (Lour.) Spreng.)
medicinal_uses: 1. Eating 3 Doddapatre leaves daily with a pinch of salt helps cure tonsillitis (swollen throat glands). 2. Half a cup of Doddapatre leaf juice mixed with a small amount of honey and pepper, consumed twice daily by children, helps cure cough, cold, and fever caused by phlegm. 3. Doddapatre leaves are applied on boils, wounds, and insect bites as a poultice. 4. Grinding the leaves and applying on joints affected by rheumatic swelling provides relief. 5. When there is unusual body odor or bad breath, chewing the leaf and applying on the body helps deodorize and applying on the forehead helps reduce headache. 6. Consuming approximately 12 grams of leaf extract daily helps treat indigestion, flatulence, urinary tract issues, uterine disorders, and other seasonal diseases. 7. Consuming the leaf extract daily helps dissolve urinary/kidney stones.
dosage_preparation: 3 leaves daily with salt for tonsillitis. Leaf juice: 1/2 cup with honey and pepper twice daily for children. Leaf extract: approximately 12 grams daily for digestive and urinary conditions. Caution: Excessive consumption of the leaf and its decoction should be avoided as it may reduce the effectiveness of the medicine.

medicinal_uses: 1. Applying the tender leaves on wounds and skin eruptions helps treat skin rashes, eczema, and various skin diseases. 2. Chewing the tender leaves helps relieve headache and dizziness.
dosage_preparation: Tender leaves applied externally on skin conditions. Leaves chewed for headache relief.

medicinal_uses: 1. Moringa leaves and drumstick pods cooked and consumed help improve eyesight, provide nutrition, and act as a general health tonic. 2. Moringa root consumed in small quantities helps cure various respiratory and digestive ailments (consuming in large amounts can be harmful, use cautiously). 3. Moringa leaves ground with pepper and garlic help treat cold and flu symptoms. 4. Moringa drumstick soup consumed regularly helps treat rheumatic pain and joint swelling; about 5 grams of drumstick root paste taken orally also helps. 5. Moringa flowers consumed in moderate portions (about 2 spoons) with honey, ghee, and other herbs help treat urinary disorders and seminal weakness. 6. Moringa bark (1/4 part) ground with appropriate herbs, consumed daily, helps treat various conditions. 7. Moringa leaf juice consumed helps treat liver and spleen enlargement. 8. Moringa root bark ground into a paste and applied on swollen glands and affected areas helps reduce inflammation and swelling (like a poultice). 9. Moringa leaves boiled in water and the extract consumed helps cure headache and cold. 10. Moringa leaves and bark decoction applied externally helps treat malarial fever and associated problems. 11. Moringa leaf juice (rasalike) mixed with a small amount of salt and pepper, consumed 3 times daily for a few days, helps dissolve kidney stones. 12. Moringa root juice (about 25 ml) mixed with 1-2 ml of salt solution, consumed twice daily with honey, helps treat various urinary tract infections. 13. Moringa leaf decoction (about 14-28 ml) mixed with honey, consumed daily and supplemented with appropriate yogurt, helps reduce abdominal fat and obesity. 14. Moringa bark juice (4 parts) mixed with 1 part sesame oil, heated together to prepare medicated oil, applied externally twice daily for 2-4 applications, helps treat earaches and ear infections. 15. Moringa leaf decoction (14-28 ml) mixed with turmeric (1 gram) and urad dal water (1 gram) consumed daily for several days helps treat back pain and body aches. 16. Moringa tree bark, drumstick shell, and other bark parts ground together (3 parts) and applied on body helps treat various skin conditions and aches from physical exertion. 17. Moringa drumstick consumed helps reduce scrotal swelling (hydrocele) when applied topically. 18. Moringa tree bark decoction consumed helps cure those affected by bad odors and foul-smelling diseases. 19. Moringa leaves and bark paste applied on the skin and joints helps prevent joint diseases and bone degeneration. 20. Juice of raw and unripe moringa fruit seeds ground and mixed with jaggery, made into small pills (2-4 pills daily) and consumed helps cure multiple neurological conditions and knee pain. 21. Seed powder mixed with ghee (about 1 gram) consumed once daily for several days helps treat bedwetting and urinary incontinence.
dosage_preparation: Root juice: about 25 ml with 1-2 ml salt solution. Leaf decoction: 14-28 ml mixed with turmeric. Bark juice and sesame oil (4:1 ratio) for medicated oil. Seed powder: 1 gram with ghee daily. Veterinary: Moringa leaf extract (one measure) mixed with pepper and water, given to cattle twice daily for 1 week to treat digestive disorders.

medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming the whole plant decoction with dried ginger powder helps treat malarial fever. 2. Consuming the plant decoction helps treat liver disorders and related conditions. 3. Consuming the plant decoction helps prevent debility and promotes overall health. 4. Consuming the whole plant decoction helps purify the blood and treat skin-related disorders. 5. Grinding the leaves with black pepper, making into a paste, mixing with water and consuming helps cure various ailments including fever, indigestion, and worm infections. The treatment is continued for 1 week for effective results.
dosage_preparation: Whole plant decoction with dried ginger for malarial fever. Leaf paste with black pepper consumed with water for 1 week.

medicinal_uses: 1. Bark decoction of the Pagade tree consumed helps treat diarrhea, dysentery (pravahika), blood in stools, and bleeding disorders. 2. Bark decoction used as a gargle helps treat dental problems and prevents teeth from loosening. 3. Chewing the green bark helps maintain dental health and prevents tooth decay. 4. Flower decoction (nasal drops) applied in the nose helps relieve headache and migraine. 5. Flower decoction consumed helps treat mumps and throat-related ailments. 6. Syrup prepared from the flowers consumed helps treat heart-related disorders and strengthens the cardiovascular system. 7. Consuming the Pagade tree fruit helps enhance virility and increase semen production.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction for oral and gargle use. Flower decoction for nasal drops and internal consumption. Fruit consumed for reproductive health.

medicinal_uses: 1. Papaya seeds mixed with honey are consumed to expel intestinal worms. 2. Applying raw papaya latex on ringworm cures the infection. 3. Consuming ripe papaya regularly helps in digestion and relieves constipation. 4. Raw papaya vegetable consumption helps enlarge the spleen (Spleen) and liver (LIVER), and cures related conditions. 5. The milky latex from the unripe fruit is useful in removing corns and warts. 6. Drinking a decoction of papaya root bark helps with kidney-related diseases and urinary problems. 7. Papaya leaf juice taken on an empty stomach serves as a liver tonic and helps with jaundice. 8. Papaya latex applied on the face removes blemishes and pimples. 9. Consuming papaya fruit regularly helps manage diabetes by controlling blood sugar levels. 10. Papaya leaf extract helps increase platelet count in dengue fever patients. 11. Consuming ripe papaya fruit aids in relieving piles and hemorrhoid symptoms. 12. Mixing 6 tola papaya seed, 6 tola pippali, and 6 tola rock salt and consuming about one tola daily for 21-42 days helps cure malarial fever and general fever.
dosage_preparation: For malarial fever: Mix 6 tola each of papaya seed, pippali, and rock salt; take one tola daily for 21-42 days. For veterinary use: Feed raw papaya fruit grated and mixed for 42 days to cure animal ailments.

medicinal_uses: 1. One gunja (approximately a small measure) each of leaf and flower powder, dissolved in betel leaf juice and consumed, relieves cough. 2. Grinding leaves and flower stalk and applying the paste helps cure skin diseases. 3. Leaf juice is given to those bitten by snakes as a remedy. 4. Consuming leaf juice helps with fever, digestive disorders of the pitta type, and hemorrhoidal conditions. 5. Leaf juice or a decoction of the bark taken internally helps with urinary problems, cold, and fever. 6. Applying paste made from Parijata bark resin on the scalp helps reduce dandruff and hair fall. 7. Grinding seeds and applying on the scalp prevents hair fall and promotes hair growth.
dosage_preparation: Leaf and flower powder: one gunja measure dissolved in betel leaf juice for cough.

medicinal_uses: 1. The root is eaten by children to cure abdominal disorders. 2. The root paste mixed with cow's ghee is applied on the body to treat leucoderma and skin conditions; it is also tied as a poultice on the forehead. 3. The root along with ashwagandha and other herbs is taken with milk to improve vitality and strength. 4. The root paste consumed with cow's milk acts as a restorative tonic for the reproductive system and improves semen quality. 5. Eating the root helps alleviate urinary tract and kidney-related conditions. 6. Consuming the root for several days improves the health of the female reproductive organs. 7. For poisonous insect bites, equal quantities of root ground with milk are consumed. 8. The plant is useful in treating sexually transmitted diseases.
dosage_preparation: Root mixed with honey and milk for vitality; root decoction in cow's milk for 41 days for reproductive health issues.

medicinal_uses: 1. The root paste is applied around the eyes like collyrium to cure eye diseases. 2. Consuming the root decoction helps with bleeding disorders, stomach problems, pregnancy weakness, urinary diseases, and strengthens the body. 3. A decoction of root and leaf is taken to cure urinary and bladder problems. 4. Consuming root and leaf decoction acts as a body strengthening tonic. 5. Steaming with a preparation of the leaves of Pattige plant, mixed with herbs, helps reduce dental problems. 6. Mixing Pattige plant root with sesame oil and applying on affected areas, consumed daily, helps treat paralysis and joint pain. The same oil can be applied externally for knee pain.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction mixed with sesame oil for external application on joints; leaf poultice for dental issues.

medicinal_uses: 1. In cases of venomous bites, the leaves of this plant are applied over the bitten area, and a leaf decoction is also taken internally. 2. A decoction of the leaves is consumed to cure jaundice. 3. For boils (gulte), swelling, and other skin eruptions, the plant leaves are ground and applied externally with positive results.
dosage_preparation: Leaf decoction taken orally for jaundice; leaf paste applied topically for venomous bites and boils.

medicinal_uses: 1. Rubbing the rhizome with a small amount of food and applying it on the chest area of children, repeating this treatment 2-3 times, helps reduce cold and cough. 2. Burning the rhizome and applying the smoke over sore areas helps relieve inflammatory swelling. 3. Consuming Baje rhizome along with ghee and honey for several days improves intelligence, memory, and reproductive capacity in children. 4. Taking 10 grams of Baje decoction orally, mixed with either warm water or honey, for 40 days helps cure epilepsy (Apasmara). 5. Grinding and applying Baje paste helps with joint pain (Sandhivata) and paralysis. 6. Putting fresh Baje juice in the ears relieves earache. Preparing a Baje-scented paste and consuming it cures loss of appetite, indigestion, abdominal distension, and stomach disorders. 7. Consuming Baje helps destroy intestinal worms. 8. Taking about 300 mg of Baje powder with 5-10 grams of Saundarya and other ingredients, mixed in warm water, helps with excessive cough and phlegm. 9. Mixing Baje, Nagakesara, pippali together in a decoction and consuming helps improve blood circulation. 10. A Baje decoction or bark decoction taken with a small amount of jaggery helps with kidney conditions and urinary blockages. In children, giving small amounts of Baje with Brahmi ghee in milk cures fever. 11. Consuming Baje is noted as safe during pregnancy in small amounts to relieve digestive issues.
dosage_preparation: Baje rhizome 10 grams decoction for 40 days for epilepsy; 300 mg powder with 5-10 grams supporting ingredients for cough; rubbed on children's chest for cold relief.

medicinal_uses: 1. Mixing the root paste with guggul and applying it topically cures all types of skin diseases. 2. Grinding Brahdande root bark and making a decoction with mutton broth or pearl millet, and consuming it, helps cure diseases related to women and menstrual disorders. 3. Taking Brahdande along with Ashwagandha and Shatavari root with milk improves vitality and sexual vigor. 4. The root decoction consumed with Kushmanda (ash gourd) extract, gandhamala, and other herbs relieves urinary disorders and fever. 5. Grinding Brahdande root bark with Ashwagandha extract and applying it on boils promotes healing. 6. Taking the root extract (4 chamcha dose) in the evening for 7 days helps eliminate intestinal worms. 7. Placing the plant's root in the eastern direction and tying its thread on the left arm, or applying root paste on the forehead, is believed to relieve headaches. 8. Brahdande root bark is ground in sesame oil and applied on affected areas for nasal ailments.
dosage_preparation: Root bark decoction: 4 chamcha dose in evening for 7 days for intestinal worms; root paste mixed with guggul for skin application.

medicinal_uses: 1. Applying Bilva leaf paste on cow dung-affected wounds on the scalp or body cures infections quickly. 2. Bilva leaf crushed finely and mixed with half a measure of curd and half a measure of decoction, combined with cow's milk; this preparation when given to children helps with vomiting, diarrhea, and digestive issues. 3. Preparing a decoction of Bilva bark and consuming it in buttermilk helps cure hemorrhoidal conditions and associated bleeding. 4. Plucking Bilva leaves, making a small paste, and applying it on the chest area reduces excessive phlegm and cough. 5. Bilva leaf juice (2 tola) mixed with curd (2 tola) consumed 2-3 times helps cure jaundice. 6. Consuming 8 chamcha of Bilva leaf juice daily for several days helps control diabetes. 7. Consuming 14-28 ml of Bilva leaf juice mixed with honey and sugar twice daily helps treat excessive urination (Prameha). 8. For Bilva fruit, taking large pieces and soaking them in water overnight, then consuming in the morning for 2-3 days helps with constipation. 9. Bilva leaf paste wrapped in cloth, dried, and given as 3-6 gram dose to children helps with diarrhea and dysentery. 10. Making a paste of Bilva fruit mixed with milk and coconut oil, applying on scalp and body for a few days helps heal skin conditions. 11. Bilva leaves boiled with sesame oil make a medicinal oil that when applied during the monsoon season cures skin issues within 5-6 days. 12. Bilva leaf powder (1 gram) mixed with 1 gram dry ginger, taken twice daily, cures hemorrhoidal (Moolavyadhi) conditions. 13. Bilva fruit consumed in buttermilk-based preparations, 1-2 tola per dose, taken twice daily helps cure diarrhea (Atisara). 14. Consuming Bilva fruit skin in small quantity with honey stops loose motions. 15. Bilva fruit pickled in sesame oil and salt is used as a digestive aid and appetizer. The oil from this preparation treats skin conditions, wounds, and various ailments.
dosage_preparation: Bilva leaf juice: 8 chamcha daily for diabetes; 14-28 ml with honey twice daily for Prameha; leaf powder 1 gram + dry ginger 1 gram twice daily for hemorrhoids; 2 tola leaf juice + 2 tola curd for jaundice 2-3 times; 3-6 gram children's dose for dysentery.

medicinal_uses: 1. The milky latex from the plant applied to scorpion stings and venomous bites neutralizes the venom. People in the area apply the leaf on the bitten area. 2. The root bark powder (about 2 grains) mixed with black pepper is given for snake bites; it induces vomiting and helps expel the venom. 3. Applying heated Ekka leaf on swollen joints provides relief from pain and swelling. 4. Ekka root bark decoction helps cure asthma and respiratory problems when taken in measured doses, twice daily for 2-5 days. 5. Applying warmed Ekka leaves on the abdomen helps with stomach pain and digestive issues. 6. Consuming measured doses of Ekka milk with other herbs for 1-2 weeks helps with chronic skin conditions. 7. The latex from the plant, when applied to warts, helps in their removal. 8-13. Various preparations including root bark, flower, and leaf decoctions are used for treating elephantiasis, dropsy, leprosy, and chronic fevers. The milky latex is applied externally for ringworm. 14-15. Ekka root decoctions taken with spices help cure paralytic conditions and improve blood circulation. 16-18. In higher doses under supervision, preparations from Ekka are used to treat asthma, liver enlargement, and spleen disorders. 25. Ekka flower preparations consumed with jaggery help cure primary infections of the reproductive system. 26. Mixing Ekka flower (1 hidi) with rock salt and making into tablets consumed for 3-4 days cures abdominal gas and bloating. 27. Taking 5-10 Ekka flowers with betel nut, salt, and eating twice daily for 1-2 times cures indigestion. 28. Applying Ekka latex on scorpion or insect bites provides immediate relief from stinging and swelling.
dosage_preparation: Root bark: 2 grain dose with black pepper for snake bites; flower with rock salt for 3-4 days for gas; 5-10 flowers with betel nut twice daily for indigestion. For veterinary use: 100 grams root pounded and given to large animals for skin diseases.

medicinal_uses: 1. Chitramula root paste mixed with equal parts of Barleria (Gokarna) leaf is applied to affected areas for treating skin ailments and swellings. 2. Chitramula root decoction taken orally helps improve digestion and cures abdominal conditions. 3. Chitramula root is an important ingredient in many traditional digestive and appetizing formulations. 4. Applying root paste on the skin helps cure chronic skin conditions, ringworm, and related infections. 5. Chitramula root mixed with ghee and other herbs is taken to improve strength and body constitution. 6. The root decoction when applied to wounds helps in faster healing and prevents infection. 7. Taking small measured doses of root powder with warm water helps relieve chronic constipation and stimulates appetite.
dosage_preparation: Root paste applied externally for skin conditions; root decoction taken orally for digestion and constipation. Caution: The plant is toxic in higher doses - only small measured doses should be used under guidance.

medicinal_uses: 1. The plant's root decoction is given to those bitten by venomous creatures as an antidote. 2. Extracting the milky sap from the plant's creeper bark, squeezing the juice, and applying it on swollen glands helps reduce the swelling. 3. The white latex from the plant, when exposed to heat, helps remove thorns embedded in the skin. 4. The flower and leaf milky sap of the plant is applied externally to cure dermatological conditions and swelling.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction for venomous bites; milky sap applied externally for glandular swellings and skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: 1. The root decoction of the plant is given to those with chronic cough for relief. 2. Root and leaf decoction when consumed helps cure blood disorders (Raktamoolavyadhi), bleeding piles (Pradara), gonorrhea-like conditions (Gonasthambhya). The decoction is also applied on wounds to prevent infection. 3. Grinding the leaves and applying as a poultice helps relieve joint pain (Sandhivata) and the pain from sprains and related swelling. 4. The leaf or root juice applied to the ears provides relief from ear pain and infections. 5. A decoction of the root consumed internally helps those with rheumatism (Sanyangi), and provides relief from muscle stiffness and joint swelling. 6. The leaf decoction applied as an enema through the rectum helps relieve piles (Bhedi) and rectal bleeding. 7. Leaf juice instilled into the rectum helps relieve anal fissures and reduces pain. 8. The leaves are ground and applied on syphilis-related and other sexually transmitted disease lesions for relief. 9. For those bitten by venomous snakes, the root or root bark decoction of this plant is consumed to provide relief from snake venom. 10. Applying the plant's root paste in milk and applying it on swellings, lumps, and growths helps reduce them. 11. Making a poultice of leaves, grinding them with sesame oil, and applying the medicated oil as anjana (eye collyrium) helps cure inflammation of the eye and urticaria.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction for cough and snake bites; leaf poultice for joint pain; leaf juice as rectal instillation for piles.

medicinal_uses: 1. Belada fruit rind (along with Tangi Beru/Cassia occidentalis) and other herbs made into a decoction, consumed with water-based preparations, helps pacify excessive heat-related disorders. 2. The bark juice (1-2 tola) mixed with a little water, honey, and sugar, consumed twice daily for 3-5 days, helps cure bleeding disorders of all kinds. 3. Belada bark juice mixed with honey and pepper, consumed with water helps reduce the intensity of hiccups. 4. Consuming Belada Panchanga (five-part preparation) helps the body gain physical endurance and longevity. 5. Purified Belada fruit gum (gondu) processed without impurities and in proper dosage helps treat skin diseases, leucoderma, blood pressure, and promotes overall healing. 6. Belada shell (fruit rind) crushed and applied on the face along with turmeric helps in reducing pimples, dark spots, and other facial blemishes. 7. Consuming Belada leaf with cold water and then smelling helps cure nausea (vishakhochi) and related conditions. 8. Belada shell paste mixed with cow's urine (Gomootra) and consumed helps cure night blindness (Ratrandhata). 9. Belada sour rind made into a decoction with sesame oil and processed into medicated oil, then applied on the forehead, helps relieve headache and also treats snake venom. 10. Belada leaf extract applied on cuts and wounds helps with faster healing. The same paste can also be used as a hair wash. 11. Belada leaf applied with rock salt and consumed helps improve appetite and taste perception. 12. Belada fruit pulp used to make sherbet and consumed helps relieve oral sores and mouth ulcers. 13. Belada fruit skin consumed with honey and sugar helps reduce excessive dental sensitivity. 14. Belada fruit skin in small doses (1/4 tola) taken regularly helps stop diarrhea. 15. Belada fruit skin taken along with jaggery and sugar helps with acidity, reduces cough, improves appetite, and promotes good health.
dosage_preparation: Bark juice 1-2 tola with honey for bleeding disorders, twice daily for 3-5 days; fruit skin 1/4 tola for diarrhea; whole plant Panchanga for longevity. For veterinary use: Belada fruit with pepper given in buttermilk to animals for digestive health.

medicinal_uses: 1. Neem bark decoction, purified and made into powder, is consumed daily for 1-2 days as an anthelmintic (to expel intestinal worms) and to cleanse the system. 2. Neem leaf decoction (1/4 lota) taken with 5 gumps of tola (a small measure) mixed with kadhuburini for several days relieves fever. 3. One tola of Neem leaf decoction mixed with sesame oil applied on the scalp once or twice a week for 1-2 weeks helps cure scalp infections (Moolavyadhi). 4. The five parts of the Neem tree (bark, leaf, flower, fruit, and root) are powdered, mixed in water or buttermilk, and consumed daily. This preparation taken nightly (1/2 to 3/4 tola) with appropriate liquid cures many ailments. This treatment continued for 3-4 months helps eradicate chronic skin diseases. 5. Neem Panchanga (five-part) decoction mixed with milk when consumed regularly cures skin diseases (Charmavyadhigalu), urinary conditions (Vaatavyadhigalu), kidney problems (Mootravyadhigalu), and eye diseases (Netraroogagalu). 6. Grinding Neem leaf with turmeric and applying on the face removes pimples and sores. 7. Consuming 3 neem leaves daily with 3 neem seeds and 3 peppercorns mixed with honey for several days relieves urinary stones. 8. Neem oil applied externally helps with joint pain. Mixed with coconut oil, it serves as an effective mosquito repellent. 9. Consuming small quantities of neem decoction regularly helps lower blood sugar in diabetic patients. 10. Neem leaf paste and turmeric applied on the body before bathing helps cure itching and other skin problems. 11. Neem twig used as a toothbrush (datun) daily helps with dental health and gum diseases. 12. Consuming Neem flower preparations helps purify blood and improves overall complexion. 13. Neem leaf juice with honey taken on an empty stomach in the morning for several weeks helps with chronic malaria.
dosage_preparation: Neem Panchanga: 1/2 to 3/4 tola nightly for 3-4 months for chronic skin diseases; leaf decoction 1/4 lota for fever; 3 leaves + 3 seeds + 3 peppers with honey for kidney stones; 5 ml Neem oil for external application in joint pain; dosages should not exceed 12-24 ml or as directed.

medicinal_uses: Note: This is an intoxicating substance; excessive use causes hallucination, delusion, mental imbalance, visual disturbance, and other harmful effects. Its controlled and measured use is recommended only under medical supervision. 1. Applying leaf juice on the scalp helps reduce hair loss and promotes hair growth. 2. Leaf juice applied to the ear helps relieve ear pain and related infections. 3. Leaf decoction mixed with sugar and kept in a covered vessel for a few days, then consumed, helps relieve body pain and acts as an analgesic. 4. Applying leaf paste on wounds or affected areas on the skin helps reduce inflammation and pain. 5. Smoking dried Bhangisoppu (hemp) in measured amounts and inhaling the smoke helps relieve rheumatic and nerve-related pains. 6. Consuming in small measured doses helps with indigestion and flatulence. 7. Applying crushed leaves on wounds helps stop bleeding and promotes clotting. 8. Consuming controlled amounts helps reduce anxiety and induces sleep in insomnia patients. 9. Bhangisoppu consumption in small doses helps reduce chronic cough. 10. It also helps as a mild laxative when taken in small quantities. For veterinary use: 1. Bhangisoppu mixed with tree bark and decoction given to animals, applied on head with ghee for livestock ailments. 2. Bhangisoppu and its preparations mixed and applied on livestock heads for controlling parasites and infections. 3. Bhangisoppu preparations applied externally on livestock wounds. 4. Bhangisoppu (10 grams) mixed with Chitramoolika (lead wort), turmeric in water, and applied as a paste on cattle nostrils for 2-3 days helps treat respiratory diseases (Soraklu).
dosage_preparation: Strictly under medical guidance only. Bhangisoppu 10 grams with Chitramoolika and turmeric for livestock respiratory ailments for 2-3 days; leaf juice topical for hair loss; controlled decoction with sugar for body pain.

medicinal_uses: 1. Consuming the root or leaf decoction helps repel insects and parasites from the body. 2. Applying root paste on wounds and sore areas helps promote healing. 3. Consuming the root decoction helps relieve body fatigue and enhances physical strength and vitality. It has nerve-tonic properties (Naapunasakta) and helps with reproductive disorders. 4. Bhimana Kaddi root in sesame oil, when consumed, helps with relief from numbness, paralysis-like conditions, and rheumatic joint pain. 5. Leaf juice applied to affected areas, especially in the mouth or on the tongue, helps reduce pain and inflammation. 6. Leaf juice consumed internally helps relieve edema (Ede sopu/swelling) and associated symptoms. 7. Consuming one leaf a day, for 40 days, is believed to have rejuvenative effects and helps strengthen the body's immune response. 8. Applying the root paste on the forehead and temples relieves headaches (Novepu). 9. The root consumed as a decoction helps with abdominal conditions, urinary disorders, and digestive complaints.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction for strength and reproductive health; one leaf daily for 40 days for rejuvenation; root paste on forehead for headache; root in sesame oil for joint pain. For veterinary use: Bhimana Kaddi juice applied on cattle skin for insect bites and wounds.

medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding 8-15 leaves of Bhoodi Soppu plant to make a paste, soaking in 1 lota of water, filtering and drinking the extract 2-3 times at intervals of 2-3 hours (Ghante), acts as a laxative that helps relieve constipation. One lota of the liquid is consumed each time as a single dose.
dosage_preparation: 8-15 leaves ground, soaked in 1 lota water, filtered and consumed 2-3 times at 2-3 hour intervals as a laxative.

medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding the leaf stalk and consuming about 10 grams placed in the abdomen helps expel gas and stomach discomfort. 2. The leaf stalk when ground and consumed helps relieve diarrhea and blood in stool. 3. The leaf stalk decoction used for washing wounds helps them heal faster. 4. Maggare fruit (unripe) ground finely and applied externally on the navel area treats abdominal bloating and related gastric conditions. 5. Consuming Maggare fruit acts as an emetic (induces vomiting) to cleanse the stomach. Consuming it in smaller amounts cures cough and reduces phlegm build-up. 6. The fruit or leaf preparations when applied on the face help in removing pimples, acne, and other facial blemishes. 7. Maggare fruit or fruit paste applied in lemon juice and then applied on the skin helps cure Herpes (Visarpa) and related skin eruptions. 8. Maggare fruit skin crushed and applied on insect bites provides relief from localized swelling, pain, and itching. 9. Maggare fruit juice applied on the scalp helps in removing lice (Henu) and prevents scalp infections. 10. Maggare fruit skin consumed as a decoction helps relieve diarrhea and loose motions.
dosage_preparation: Leaf stalk: 10 grams for gas relief; fruit paste with lemon juice for Herpes; fruit decoction for diarrhea; fruit skin crushed for insect bite relief.

medicinal_uses: 1. Maragade leaf decoction mixed with buttermilk is consumed to help determine the exact cause of illness and then the appropriate quantity of the herb is decided and given daily, three times for weak patients. 2. The leaf or root decoction or bark decoction is consumed to repel intestinal parasites and worms.
dosage_preparation: Leaf decoction in buttermilk three times daily for weak patients; leaf/root/bark decoction for intestinal parasites.

medicinal_uses: 1. Muttuga flowers and bark decoction is used for treating skin conditions and is a traditional blood purifier. The flowers soaked in water for 12 hours provide a red dye. 2. Muttuga bark and 3 types of other bark preparations mixed with specific herbs are used to treat chronic skin diseases like eczema and psoriasis. The decoction is consumed along with a special diet for 2 months. 3. Muttuga gum (Butea gum/Bengal kino) mixed with specific herbs is used for treating diarrhea and dysentery. 4. Consuming a decoction of Muttuga bark serves as an anthelmintic to expel intestinal worms, especially tapeworms. 5. Muttuga seed paste applied externally helps cure ringworm and related skin infections. 6. Muttuga flowers boiled and the water used for bathing helps improve skin health and complexion. 7. Muttuga bark decoction consumed along with other herbs helps treat liver disorders and improves liver function. 8. The seed paste applied on affected areas helps relieve joint swelling and arthritis pain. 9. Muttuga leaf juice applied on wounds helps in faster healing. 10. The flowers consumed as a vegetable or in preparations help purify blood and improve overall health. 11. Muttuga gum mixed with buttermilk is used to treat chronic diarrhea. 12. Muttuga seed paste (1/2 tola) mixed with Triphala and ghee is given to children twice daily for 2-3 days to expel intestinal worms.
dosage_preparation: Seed paste 1/2 tola with Triphala and ghee twice daily for 2-3 days for worms in children; bark decoction with diet for 2 months for chronic skin diseases; gum in buttermilk for chronic diarrhea. For veterinary use: Muttuga bark decoction along with specific herbs given to livestock for removing parasites.

medicinal_uses: 1. Fresh leaves are applied as a poultice on boils and swellings to promote suppuration. 2. Rubbing leaf juice on gum ulcers and wounds heals them. 3. Leaf paste applied with turmeric helps cure dental ailments and tooth pain. 4. Leaves applied on the body above joints help reduce pain and swelling caused by arthritis. 5. The bark decoction from the tree trunk helps cure eye-related diseases. 6. Leaf juice (2 spoons) mixed with honey consumed daily helps destroy intestinal worms. 7. Leaf juice mixed with a little salt applied externally and consumed internally helps control excessive bleeding and diarrhea.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice 2 spoons with honey for intestinal worms. Leaf paste mixed with turmeric for dental applications.

medicinal_uses: 1. A decoction of the roots consumed in 1-2 spoon doses helps relieve gonorrhea. 2. Root decoction or bark decoction consumed helps cure stomach ache. 3. Fresh leaf juice consumed daily helps relieve constipation and urinary blockage. 4. Whole plant decoction consumed helps cure snake and scorpion venom. 5. Fresh juice or paste from the plant taken internally helps stop excessive menstrual bleeding. 6. Leaves are ground and tied as a bandage on swollen areas to reduce swelling.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction in 1-2 spoon doses for gonorrhea. Fresh leaf juice daily for constipation.

medicinal_uses: 1. Root decoction mixed with buttermilk consumed helps cure blood-related disorders and piles. 2. Root bark ground in water, filtered (keeping 1/2 portion), and mixed with liquid extract, consumed twice daily for 41 days improves skin complexion and prevents various skin ailments. 3. Root bark ground and mixed with ginger taken for 2-5 days relieves stomach ache; also useful for treating bloating and gas. Seeds consumed also have the same effect. 4. Hair oil prepared with the plant's decoction and mixed with sesame oil, when applied regularly, promotes hair growth. 5. The plant's root bark powder mixed with honey and turmeric consumed helps cure throat and tonsil disorders. 6. The plant's root bark powder consumed in milk helps relieve inflammation. 7. The plant sap consumed helps treat jaundice, anemia, stomach-related ailments and similar conditions. 8. Whole plant root paste applied in the nostril as a poultice and inhaled helps improve vitality. 9. Leaves boiled in water and consumed as paste for about 20 grams daily helps cure skin diseases and ulcers. 10. Blood with a reddish color or white discharge conditions (Hemicrania - daytime headache) can be treated by consuming the appropriate preparations. 11. Regular consumption of the plant root decoction helps purify blood and remove skin diseases.
dosage_preparation: Root bark extract: 2 drops (10 ml) twice daily for 41 days. Root paste about 20 grams daily for skin diseases. 2-5 days treatment for stomach ache.

medicinal_uses: 1. Leaf decoction along with castor oil and spices taken internally and externally treats rheumatic conditions. Heating leaves with 1 pinch of turmeric and tying over joints relieves joint pain (2-3 days treatment). 2. Leaves heated and placed on the forehead treats headache. Leaves bandaged over painful areas provide relief (2-3 hours). 3. Root bark (1 inch piece), dry ginger, pepper, mixed with water, boiled and reduced to half, consumed twice daily treats fever. 4. Leaf or seed oil applied to skin eruptions, boils, and ulcers heals them. Leaf oil massage helps with paralysis. 5. Plant's fresh juice (1 spoon) mixed with honey (1/2 spoon), or castor oil applied with the juice helps treat venereal (sexually transmitted) diseases. 6. Root juice (3-4 drops) applied in the ears treats ear-related ailments. 7. Seeds taken internally act as a vermifuge (expelling intestinal worms). 8. Tender leaves or flowers consumed as vegetable help treat menstrual disorders. 9. Fresh leaves ground and applied on wounds and ulcers heal them. 10. Leaves boiled in oil and applied cure rheumatic and arthritic conditions. 11. Leaf decoction with salt as a gargle cures mouth ulcers and gum diseases. 12. Flower decoction treats diarrhea and liver disorders. 13. Leaf juice of about 5 grams taken with 1 cup water treats malarial fever. 14. Leaf juice (14-28 ml) consumed thrice daily treats goiter (thyroid swelling). Root juice applied externally on swellings reduces them. 15. Leaf decoction (14-28 ml) with honey consumed thrice daily cures chronic fever. 16. Leaf decoction applied on the scalp relieves dandruff, headache, and itching. 17. Lakki leaves with sesame oil, bones meal, garlic, consumed cooked with buttermilk and rice cures hemorrhoids when treatment is continued 1-2 times. 18. Lakki leaf poultice (about 5 leaves) placed slightly heated on the navel area helps in cases of uterine prolapse. Treatment should be done once for two days, continued for one week alternately. 19. Lakki fresh juice combined with sesame oil heated and applied, using this for 1-2 months helps resolve chronic skin conditions. 20. Lakki juice (4 spoons) mixed with 2 spoons of cow's ghee consumed helps treat flatulence/wind-related ailments. 21. A fistful of lakki leaves soaked in water overnight, strained and consumed 2-3 times daily cures rheumatic joint pain.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice 14-28 ml thrice daily for goiter. Root bark 1 inch piece with dry ginger and pepper boiled for fever. Lakki juice 4 spoons with 2 spoons ghee for flatulence. Leaf decoction 14-28 ml with honey thrice daily for fever.

medicinal_uses: 1. Leaf juice consumed helps treat indigestion. 2. Leaf juice or bark decoction consumed helps cure urinary disorders. Consuming excessively can cause intoxication; moderate use is recommended for urinary problems and diabetes. 3. Flower juice has emetic and expectorant properties; consuming it induces vomiting and helps expel phlegm. 4. The ripe fruit consumed helps relieve excessive thirst.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice or bark decoction for urinary disorders, used in moderation.

latin_name: Vetiveria zizanioides (L.) Nash. = Andropogon muricatus Retz.
medicinal_uses: 1. Grinding the root and drinking the decoction helps relieve stomach ache and fever; it also brings down body temperature. 2. Root gum or powder applied externally helps reduce skin irritation and itching. 3. About 5-10 gunja weight of the root decoction mixed with milk or cold water consumed 1-2 times helps treat bleeding disorders (blood in stools). 4. The root scent when applied acts as an insect repellent, reducing skin itching and irritation; it also keeps away mosquitoes and harmful organisms. 5. The root soaked in water overnight, filtered, and consumed daily helps cool the body temperature; water becomes fragrant and tasty. Tamil Nadu people also follow this practice. 6. Root decoction consumed helps relieve vomiting and dysentery. 7. Root bark and sesame oil heated together and prepared as hair oil, when applied to the hair and scalp, provides cooling effect and nourishes hair.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction 5-10 gunja weight with milk or cold water 1-2 times for bleeding disorders. Roots soaked overnight in water for daily cooling drink.

latin_name: Lantana camara L. = L. aculeata L.
medicinal_uses: 1. Lantana plant leaves are ground and applied with turmeric on itchy skin or wounds to provide relief and heal the skin condition. 2. In Guinea, a decoction of this plant is used to treat rheumatic joint diseases and malaria.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste with turmeric for external application on wounds and itchy skin.

medicinal_uses: 1. Root bark or leaf decoction consumed helps relieve joint inflammation. 2. Root decoction or bark decoction consumed helps relieve urinary tract infections and kidney stone problems. 3. Root bark decoction given to those with rheumatic conditions helps cure inflammation. 4. Stem bark consumed with water in a cold form helps treat eye disorders affecting vision.
dosage_preparation: Root bark decoction for joint inflammation and urinary conditions.

latin_name: Delonix elata (L.) Gamble = Poinciana elata L.
medicinal_uses: 1. Root bark powder or leaf decoction consumed helps relieve rheumatic conditions and abdominal disorders. 2. The tree's bark decoction consumed helps cure fever and reduces body inflammation. 3. Root bark or leaf consumed as decoction helps relieve urinary tract problems and kidney-related stone issues.
dosage_preparation: Root bark powder or leaf decoction for rheumatic conditions.

medicinal_uses: 1. Roots are cut, fried in sesame oil, and the oil from the heated plant used externally to reduce swelling and cure skin diseases. 2. Roots ground and applied on bites or stings from poisonous creatures help neutralize venom. 3. Leaves are heated in water, and after cooling, the water is used for bathing to treat body rashes and skin disorders. 4. Leaf decoction mixed with sugar consumed helps treat sexually transmitted and reproductive system diseases. 5. Fresh leaf juice applied in the nostrils helps stop nosebleeds and cures blood-related conditions. 6. Consuming 3-4 leaves daily helps control diabetes. 7. Whole plant root or decoction consumed helps cure fever, especially intermittent fever. In veterinary use: 1. Vishamadhari plant (3 fistfuls) cut and ground with turmeric powder, mixed with areca nut and applied helps treat cattle skin parasites. 2. Vishamadhari plant is used in cattle diseases and veterinary medicine; treatment is done systematically following traditional methods.
dosage_preparation: 3-4 fresh leaves daily for diabetes. Root oil preparation for external application on swellings. Leaf decoction with sugar for reproductive disorders.

medicinal_uses: 1. Root decoction consumed acts as a gentle purgative, similar to mild laxative action. 2. Root bark powder placed on the tongue and swallowed with milk helps strengthen the body and improve vitality. 3. Root scent inhaled helps cure nasal congestion and running nose. 4. Leaves ground and applied on boils and skin irritations help them heal faster. 5. Leaves cut and tied as a bandage on wounds help the wound heal and reduce the foul smell. 6. Vibhuti plant powder or root bark ground with sesame oil and applied to the scalp for several days helps cure dandruff and promotes hair growth.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction as a gentle purgative. Root bark ground with sesame oil for scalp application over several days.

medicinal_uses: 1. For headaches caused by fever, grinding the leaves and applying them as a poultice on the forehead provides relief. 2. Root scent, raw fruit, or bark decoction consumed helps treat piles and hemorrhoids. 3. Excessive consumption of the tree's fruit can cause increased thirst, heart palpitations, and similar health conditions. 4. Leaf juice mixed with milk and sugar, consumed, helps treat urinary disorders, swelling, and diabetic conditions. 5. The tree's fruit consumption helps in increasing urination and acts as a diuretic. 6. The tree's fruit consumed helps improve sperm production. 7. Root bark decoction consumed after delivery helps prevent uterine complications and related postpartum problems. 8. Root bark paste prepared with oil, applied on scalp, helps cure dandruff and promote hair growth. 9. Root bark and fruit consumed helps treat nerve-related weakness and pain.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice with milk and sugar for urinary disorders. Root bark decoction for postpartum care.

medicinal_uses: 1. Whole plant decoction consumed helps cure fever.
dosage_preparation: Whole plant decoction for fever.

medicinal_uses: 1. Sahadevi plant root paste applied on itchy swollen areas and consumed also helps relieve pain and itching. 2. The plant juice or bark decoction consumed helps purify blood and treat skin-related ailments. 3. Root bark decoction consumed helps relieve fever, and also cures skin diseases. 4. Fresh juice consumed helps treat urinary problems and clears kidney-related issues. 5. Leaf juice (5-10 ml) consumed helps kill intestinal worms. 6. Flower decoction (approximately 30-60 gunje weight) or root decoction (approximately 15-60 gunje weight) consumed helps destroy parasitic worms. 7. In traditional practice, Sahadevi plant mixed with other herbs, bark, pepper, cumin and other spices ground together and consumed with one glass of mixed decoction daily helps treat skin diseases like psoriasis. 8. Seed paste applied on the scalp helps kill head lice.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice 5-10 ml for intestinal worms. Flower decoction 30-60 gunje weight for parasites. Root decoction 15-60 gunje weight for worms.

medicinal_uses: Note: The seed powder is toxic. It can cause eye damage and vision loss if it contacts the eyes; this has been mentioned by Kirtikar and other authors in their reference works. Use with caution. 1. Leaves are boiled in water, ground into a paste, and applied on wounds and boils to promote faster healing and draws out suppuration. 2. The unripe fruit pulp ground and applied on the gums and teeth helps relieve toothache and dental pain. 3. Fresh and tender custard apple leaves when consumed help treat diarrhea and dysentery (bloody stools). 4. Seeds are ground and mixed with turmeric, then applied as a paste on affected areas to help treat skin infections and boils. 5. Fruit rind decoction prepared and consumed when having fever helps reduce elevated body temperature. 6. Crushed leaves placed on the nose of an unconscious person help revive them and restore consciousness. 7. Seed paste applied on the head helps kill lice and promotes cleanliness. 8. Leaves ground and applied as a face pack helps improve skin complexion and keeps the skin on the face smooth. 9. Custard apple leaves ground with turmeric and applied on affected areas for 7-14 days treats boils and infections. 10. Root bark consumed daily helps stop loose stools and dysentery. 11. Tree bark decoction consumed helps relieve loose motions/diarrhea.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste on wounds for healing. Seed paste with turmeric for skin infections. Leaf paste with turmeric applied for 7-14 days for boils.

medicinal_uses: 1. Bitter gourd consumed in various preparations helps treat digestive disorders, improves appetite, cleans the intestines, and helps manage worm infestations. Used regularly in diet. 2. Fresh bitter gourd juice (about 1 cup) with honey consumed every morning (10-12 days) or for 7 days with buttermilk helps treat piles and hemorrhoids. 3. Bitter gourd preparations help treat worm infections in children and adults. Regular consumption is beneficial. 4. Bitter gourd leaf juice with cumin, jaggery, consumed for about 2 teaspoons helps treat constipation. Ground preparation taken for 3-7 days relieves chronic constipation. 5. Bitter gourd juice consumed regularly helps treat diabetes and high blood sugar conditions. 6. Bitter gourd curry made with various spices helps reduce joint pain and rheumatic conditions. 7. Bitter gourd curry prepared with sesame oil and cumin helps treat skin diseases. 8. Bitter gourd leaf juice applied externally on skin conditions helps heal chronic itchy rashes; applying it for about an hour and then washing provides relief. 9. Bitter gourd leaf juice applied to the eyes helps treat night blindness (applied for 7 days). This treatment has been referenced in traditional texts. 10. Bitter gourd seeds (hom 9), naga (hom 9), and similar combinations in equal proportions consumed help treat digestive disorders and flatulence. 11. Bitter gourd (root) juice along with equal amounts of bitter leaf juice consumed (1-2 times daily) helps relieve skin diseases. Bitter gourd fruit consumed with kaala rasa (dark liquid preparations) and other spices also provides benefits. 12. Bitter gourd juice (1 pavu) mixed with appropriate amount of coconut milk, consumed at regular intervals helps cure worm infestations. Do this treatment for about fifteen days continuously. 13. Bitter gourd stems and leaves taken in equal proportions, ground and applied as poultice on ringworm, helps cure it. 14. Bitter gourd unripe leaf juice applied on inflamed areas helps reduce swelling. 15. Bitter gourd plant leaves applied on wounds and then bandaged helps draw out infections and reduces inflammation. 16. Bitter gourd stems consumed helps treat snake venom and poisoning. 17. Regular consumption of about 5 grams each of bitter gourd leaf, curry leaf, kanthari leaf, tulasi leaf combined with appropriate spices helps treat diabetes and related metabolic conditions.
dosage_preparation: Fresh juice 1 cup with honey every morning for 10-12 days for piles. 5 grams each of bitter gourd leaf with curry leaf, kanthari and tulasi for diabetes daily.

medicinal_uses: 1. The root paste applied on calluses (corns) helps soften and remove them. 2. In cases of whooping cough, the plant extract mixed with buttermilk and salt is given to drink. The root paste is also applied on wind-related swellings to get relief. 3. Whole plant root extract consumed with buttermilk helps treat urinary disorders and promotes urination. 4. The plant sap is given to children to treat intestinal worms.
dosage_preparation: Root paste applied on corns for removal. Plant extract with buttermilk for whooping cough.

medicinal_uses: 1. Honagonne root decoction (2 tola) with 1/4 seeru of milk consumed on an empty stomach daily helps increase urine output smoothly. 2. Honagonne plant's fresh leaves consumed as a vegetable after child delivery helps improve lactation and appetite within 8 days. 3. Honagonne plant fresh juice (2 tola) mixed with mullangi (radish) fresh juice (2 tola) consumed together with a small amount of rock candy helps in dissolving kidney stones; consuming this daily for 2-3 weeks helps cure urinary tract calculi. 4. In Honagonne plant's juice, the addition of buttermilk and lime helps in clearing eye irritation and prevents cataracts. 5. Honagonne plant (along with root) 1 seeru mixed with gingelly oil (1 pavu), sesame oil and other ingredients, boiled well together and the resulting oil applied to muscles helps strengthen muscles, bones and improves hair growth. 6. Honagonne plant leaf (2 handfuls) taken and ground with turmeric root, applied evenly on all types of wounds helps promote healing of all external injuries. 7. Honagonne root juice (1 tola) mixed with a small amount of pepper and a little ghee consumed helps treat blood-related disorders. 8. Honagonne root bark in milk consumed helps cure urinary tract conditions and related ailments. Treatment should be continued for 3 days. 9. Honagonne fresh juice (2 tola) mixed with mulangi fresh juice (2 tola) and one pinch of sugar, consumed daily for 2-3 weeks helps dissolve kidney stones and treats urinary calculi. 10. Honagonne plant fresh leaves or paste consumed helps improve vision and prevents eye-related weaknesses. 11. Honagonne fresh juice consumed (one fist) daily helps treat various eye conditions. Applying the juice over the eyelids and consuming it also provides additional benefits for eye health.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction 2 tola with 1/4 seeru milk daily for diuretic effect. Fresh juice 2 tola with radish juice 2 tola for kidney stones over 2-3 weeks. About 5-6 fistfuls of plant with water consumed for 4-5 days for urinary issues (veterinary use).

latin_name: Spilanthes clava DC. = S. acmella auct non (L.) Murr.
medicinal_uses: 1. Flower buds chewed in the mouth help relieve toothache and provide numbness that helps reduce dental pain. The plant also helps cure tonsillitis and throat-related disorders. 2. Whole plant root soaked in water overnight, filtered, and consumed next day helps relieve diarrhea and loose motions. 3. Skin conditions are treated by applying the plant paste over affected areas and using it as a wash, which helps remove skin infections. 4. Leaf juice or decoction consumed helps treat eye diseases (especially psoriasis-like conditions), and applying on skin helps cure quickly. 5. Root bark decoction consumed acts as an antidiarrheal agent. 6. Leaf decoction consumed helps treat conditions where there is swelling in joints; applied as a poultice or paste on inflamed areas it helps reduce joint swelling and pain.
dosage_preparation: Flower buds chewed directly for toothache. Root soaked overnight in water for diarrhea.

latin_name: Pongamia pinnata (L.) Pierre = P. glabra Vent.
medicinal_uses: 1. Honge seed oil applied on skin eruptions, rashes, and chronic skin diseases helps cure them. Mixing it with lemon juice and applying helps treat ringworm and fungal infections. 2. Honge bark ground into fine powder, mixed with water, applied on affected areas helps treat various skin ailments. 3. Honge leaf decoction used for bathing helps treat scabies and other itchy skin conditions. 4. Honge seed oil mixed with equal parts of coconut oil applied on affected skin areas helps treat eczema and dermatitis conditions. 5. Honge bark, along with Dodda mara bark, Hoovarasi bark, and other ingredients taken in equal proportions (about 100 grams each), ground with 50 grams of turmeric, cumin, cloves, and salt, boiled in water to make a decoction, consuming this helps treat skin diseases, boils, and chronic ulcers. 6. Honge branches heated and used to press against children's gums helps relieve teething pain; the sap from heated branches applied to the gums of babies provides soothing relief. 7. Honge seed oil preparation (karpura) applied on thick-skinned areas of hands and feet helps soften them and cure cracked skin conditions.
dosage_preparation: Honge seed oil mixed with lemon juice for ringworm. Combined bark decoction (100g each of multiple barks) with 50g turmeric, cumin, cloves and salt for chronic skin diseases.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating recurrent tympany in buffaloes, chronic gastritis, and as an anti-tympanic agent. The essential oil has antibacterial and antifungal properties, effective against various pathogens. Also used for managing lichen planus and as a digestive aid.
dosage_preparation: The seeds are used in decoction or powder form. Essential oil is extracted and used in various preparations.

medicinal_uses: Used for wound healing and as a hemostatic agent to stop bleeding. Has hepatoprotective activity, hair growth promoting properties, and antibiotic efficacy. The plant extract promotes wound healing and has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf juice is applied topically for wounds. Decoction of the whole plant is used internally.

medicinal_uses: Used as a remedy for skin diseases and inflammation. The whole plant decoction with 5 grams of powder is applied to skin ailments for 2 days. Also useful for treating boils and swellings when applied externally.
dosage_preparation: Decoction of leaves is applied to affected areas. Leaf paste is used externally for skin conditions. Root powder (5 gm) mixed with buttermilk is taken internally.

medicinal_uses: Used for its pharmacological actions including antifertility effects and insect growth regulatory properties. It has antinematocidal activity and is used in traditional medicine as a parasitic herb with various healing properties.
dosage_preparation: The whole plant is used in decoction form. Fresh juice or paste is applied externally.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating goitre by applying the plant paste on the throat area. Also effective for piles (hemorrhoids) when used as a poultice. The whole plant is used in preparations for digestive disorders and as a cooling agent.
dosage_preparation: Half a handful of plant material is crushed and mixed with buttermilk for internal use. Plant poultice applied externally for goitre treatment for 3 days, repeated for 40 days. For piles, the plant is made into suppositories.

name_variants: ಕಟ್ಟುಗೊಂಡ ಫಲ, ತೊಟ್ಟ, ತೇನೊಟ, ತೊಂಟ್ಟಲ, ತೊಂಟ್ಟಲು, ಮುಧುಕಕಲ್ಲಾಣಿ, ಮುಳ್ಳುಹಾಕೆ, ಲಂಭೀಕಾಯಿ
medicinal_uses: Root and bark decoctions are used for treating skin diseases and rheumatic conditions. The plant has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Used traditionally for treating boils, swellings, and as a counter-irritant when applied externally.
dosage_preparation: Root bark decoction given 3-4 times daily. Fresh root bark paste applied externally for skin conditions. Bark powder (1/4 spoon) mixed with warm water for internal use 2-3 times daily.

medicinal_uses: Root decoctions are used for treating skin diseases and rheumatic pain. The plant is used to treat bleeding, diarrhea and dysentery. Root paste mixed with milk is given for body pain and joint inflammation.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction of 5 grams in 100 ml water, reduced to half. Root paste with milk or honey given 1-2 times daily. External application of leaf paste for swellings.

latin_name: Madhuca longifolia (L.) Macbride var. latifolia (Roxb.) A. Chevalier
medicinal_uses: The flowers are used to make alcoholic beverages and the oil from seeds is used for skin care. The bark has anti-inflammatory properties and is used in treating rheumatism. Seed oil (Mahua oil) is used for skin diseases and as a laxative. The tree has anti-nematocidal and bactericidal properties.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction for internal use. Seed oil applied externally for skin conditions. Flower extract used as a tonic.

latin_name: Merremia tridentata (L.) Hallier f. ssp. hastata (Desr.) Oostr.
medicinal_uses: Used for treating constipation, piles (hemorrhoids), and skin diseases. The root decoction is useful for relieving joint pain and rheumatic conditions. Also applied externally as a poultice for swellings and inflammatory conditions.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction (10 grams in 100 ml water, reduced to half) taken 1-2 times daily. For constipation, 2-3 grams of root powder with warm water. For piles, root paste applied externally as poultice. Treatment continued for 3-4 days up to 40 days.
