medicinal_uses: The plant has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It shows in vitro cytotoxic and antitumor activity. The fresh juice is applied on wounds for quick healing and used to treat eye inflammations. Also useful for treating diarrhea and dysentery.
dosage_preparation: Fresh plant juice (half cup) taken internally 2 times with equal amount of water, 3-4 times daily for quick recovery. Leaf juice applied on filter paper and used as poultice for wounds.

medicinal_uses: Onion has hypolipidemic effects reducing cholesterol and blood lipids. It has antimicrobial activity against various pathogens including fungi. Used traditionally for treating respiratory conditions, earache, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The essential oil has antifungal activity and the bulb extract has anti-atherosclerotic effects.
dosage_preparation: Raw onion juice or cooked onion consumed regularly. Onion juice applied in ear for earache. Onion paste applied externally for boils and insect bites.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating diarrhea, dysentery, and diabetes. Has anti-diarrhoeal, antispasmodic, and hypoglycemic properties. The fruit has antispasmodic activity and is used in Diarex formulation. Bark is used for treating stomach disorders and intestinal worms.
dosage_preparation: Root bark paste applied externally for skin conditions. Fruit powder (5-10 grams) taken with water for diarrhea treatment for 40 days. Bark decoction with honey and sugar for dysentery, taken 3-4 times daily.

latin_name: Cyanotis tuberosa (Roxb.) Schultes and Schultes f.
medicinal_uses: The tuber is used traditionally for various medicinal purposes. In Ayurveda, a decoction of 500 grams of the tuber is made and used. The plant is used for treating joint conditions and as a nutritive tonic.
dosage_preparation: Tuber decoction (500 grams) is prepared for internal use as described in traditional Ayurvedic practice.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating dysentery, diarrhea, and stye (Hordeolum). The fruit has anxiolytic effects and the bark is used in antifungal preparations. Fruit decoctions are used to treat digestive disorders. Also has antifungal and antimicrobial properties against rice blast pathogen (Pyricularia grisea).
dosage_preparation: Root bark decoction for dysentery taken 3-4 times daily. Fruit decoction (20 grams of fruit with 10 grams of bark) boiled and strained, taken once daily for 3-4 days. Leaf decoction applied on stye for relief.

medicinal_uses: Cardamom seeds are used as a digestive aid and carminative. The essential oil has antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Used in Ayurvedic eye-drop preparations and for treating vaginitis and cervicitis. Has anti-hepatotoxic activity and is used in formulations like Kamilari for liver protection.
dosage_preparation: Seeds powdered and used in various Ayurvedic formulations. Essential oil used in herbal eye drops preparation. Sahacharadi thaila preparation with cardamom used for oligospermia.

medicinal_uses: A well-known brain tonic used to improve memory, learning, and cognitive function. Has immunostimulant activity, wound healing properties, and anti-ulcer effects. Used in treatment of epilepsy, schizophrenia, and as a psychotropic drug. The plant extract shows anti-filarial, antiulcer, and adaptogenic properties.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf juice taken regularly as a brain tonic. Whole plant decoction for various internal conditions. Topical formulations used for wound healing. Marketed as Madecassol for peptic ulcer treatment.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating burns and skin inflammations. The plant juice applied on burns provides cooling relief. Also used for treating digestive complaints and as a laxative. The mucilaginous leaves are applied as poultice for inflammatory conditions.
dosage_preparation: Fresh plant juice applied on burns for relief. For digestive issues, plant decoction taken daily for 40 days. The whole plant cooked as vegetable is consumed regularly for its nutritive value.

medicinal_uses: Has antiplasmodial activity and is used for treating malaria caused by Plasmodium falciparum. Leaf extract has been found effective against poliomyelitis and measles. Used traditionally for treating fever, skin diseases, and as an anti-inflammatory agent.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction (20 grams root bark, 10 grams leaf mixed with 5 grams other herbs) boiled in water, taken after straining. Treatment for fever continued for 10-15 minutes steaming followed by 2-3 days of rest. Leaf paste applied externally for skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating ringworm (dermatophytosis) and herpes skin infections. Has antiurolithic (kidney stone dissolving) activity and antibacterial properties. The leaf paste is applied externally on skin infections and the plant is used in traditional Pashanabedha formulations for kidney stones.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf paste applied on ringworm-affected areas for several days. Plant decoction (1 gram each of plant, pepper, and turmeric in 100 ml water) taken internally. For kidney stones, decoction of 500 grams plant material with other herbs is prepared.

medicinal_uses: Used traditionally as an orchid herb with medicinal properties. The plant is used in preparations for treating joint inflammation and as a general tonic. The pseudobulbs contain bioactive compounds used in traditional medicine.
dosage_preparation: Plant material (10 grams) boiled with other ingredients (10 grams each) in water, strained and taken. Decoction used for 2-3 months for chronic conditions.

medicinal_uses: The rhizome is used for its hepatoprotective activity, protecting against liver injury. It has anti-eosinophilia effects and is used in treating gastrointestinal disorders. The plant also shows antifungal activity and cytotoxic properties against certain cancer cell lines.
dosage_preparation: Rhizome powder 10 grams boiled in water, decoction taken 2-3 times; also used as paste for external application.

medicinal_uses: The bulb is used in the management of congestive cardiac failure as an Ayurvedic drug. The paste of the bulb is applied externally for treating warts and corns. It also has diuretic properties and is used in traditional medicine for dropsy and related conditions.
dosage_preparation: Bulb paste applied externally on warts; also prepared as decoction for internal use.

medicinal_uses: The leaf juice is used for treating wounds on livestock and for healing maggot-infested wounds. The fresh leaf paste is applied on the scalp to treat dandruff and lice infestation. The plant also has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf juice applied externally on wounds; leaf paste applied on scalp for 1-2 days for parasitic conditions.

medicinal_uses: The plant paste is used externally to treat joint inflammation and arthritis. A decoction of the root and leaves combined with other herbs is used to reduce fever and treat digestive disorders. The plant has demonstrated anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties in pharmacological studies.
dosage_preparation: Half teaspoon of paste mixed with appropriate vehicle; root and leaf decoction taken 1 part with honey, 1 part daily for 6 days for recovery.

medicinal_uses: Catharanthus roseus is widely known for its anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine, used in treating leukemia and Hodgkin's disease. The plant also has hypoglycemic properties, and leaf extracts are used traditionally for diabetes management. It exhibits antibacterial, antifungal, and insecticidal activities.
dosage_preparation: Leaf extract or decoction used; alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine are extracted for pharmaceutical use in cancer chemotherapy.

medicinal_uses: The fruits are used to treat blood purification disorders and have antifungal properties. The fruit and bark decoction is used for treating skin diseases and as a remedy for digestive problems. The plant also has hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory activities.
dosage_preparation: Fruit consumed directly 5-10 at a time; bark decoction prepared with water for skin disorders; paste applied on affected areas for 4-8 days.

medicinal_uses: The tuber and leaf petiole are used to treat alopecia (hair loss) by applying warmed leaf preparations on affected areas for 1-2 months. The corm is used as a remedy for piles (hemorrhoids) and the leaf juice is applied to stop bleeding. The plant has cholesterol-lowering properties when consumed as part of diet.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste heated and applied for alopecia for 1-2 months; corm decoction taken internally for digestive disorders; leaves consumed as vegetable for nutritional benefits.

medicinal_uses: The bark has significant antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant is used as a febrifuge (fever reducer) and has shown hepatoprotective activity. Stem bark preparations are used as adulterants of Ashoka bark in traditional medicine for treating gynecological disorders.
dosage_preparation: Bark pieces 100 meters boiled in water, decoction taken 25 ml doses, 5 times daily; bark powder mixed with other ingredients for specific formulations.

medicinal_uses: The plant is extensively used for treating bronchial asthma and respiratory disorders. The latex has antidiarrhoeal and anti-amoebic properties. It is used externally for treating skin diseases including warts, and has demonstrated antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifertility activities in pharmacological studies.
dosage_preparation: Whole plant decoction; fresh plant juice taken internally; latex applied on warts externally.

medicinal_uses: The gum (Mocharasa) is used in traditional medicine for treating diarrhea and dysentery. Bark preparations are used for menorrhagia and other gynecological disorders. The plant has hypotensive and hypoglycemic properties, and the thorns are used in treating inflammatory conditions and rheumatoid arthritis.
dosage_preparation: Various Ayurvedic formulations including Raktarsha and Mocharasa yoga preparations; bark decoction and gum preparations used internally.

medicinal_uses: The leaf paste is applied externally on patches affected by alopecia (hair loss), used daily for 15-20 days to promote hair regrowth. The plant is also used as a remedy for ringworm infections, where the leaf application over 15-20 days helps in recovery.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf paste applied on affected patches daily for 15-20 days for alopecia; same application method for ringworm treatment.

medicinal_uses: The plant is used for wound healing - hence the Kannada name 'Gayamari Gida' (wound-healing plant). The root and leaf paste is applied on cuts, wounds and boils to promote healing. It is also used for treating snake bites and scorpion stings in folk medicine.
dosage_preparation: Root and leaf paste applied externally on wounds; juice extracted and administered for snake bites.

medicinal_uses: The plant is used in treating skin disorders and the seeds have been studied for their pyrrolizidine alkaloid content. The dried seed pods make a rattling sound, giving the plant its Kannada name. The whole plant is used as a green manure and has soil-enriching nitrogen-fixing properties.
dosage_preparation: Plant paste applied externally; seed decoction used after careful preparation due to alkaloid content.

medicinal_uses: The plant is a renowned brain tonic (Medhya Rasayana) in Ayurveda, used to improve memory and cognitive function. Root paste is used for treating alopecia and hair problems. The plant has demonstrated anti-diabetic properties, lowering serum sugar levels, and has cholinergic activity that enhances memory and central nervous system function.
dosage_preparation: 3 grams root powder with half cup milk twice daily for memory; root paste applied externally for skin conditions; leaf decoction taken for 8-10 days for various conditions.

medicinal_uses: The plant leaf paste is applied on boils and abscesses to reduce swelling and promote healing. The root decoction is used for treating toothache and gum problems. The plant also has anti-inflammatory properties and is used in folk medicine for treating joint pain.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste mixed with quarter portion each of lime juice and turmeric, applied externally for 4-5 days; root decoction used as gargle for dental problems 2-3 days.

medicinal_uses: The marking nut is a key drug in Ayurveda (Bhallataka) used extensively in treating rheumatoid arthritis. The nut milk extract has demonstrated hepatoprotective, anticancer, and antioxidant properties. It is also used for treating skin diseases including leucoderma and vitiligo, and has shown nematicidal and antifertility activities.
dosage_preparation: Nut processed carefully to remove toxic resin before use; oil applied externally for skin conditions; Bhallataka preparations used in Ayurvedic formulations for arthritis treatment.

medicinal_uses: The cashew nut shell liquid (CNSL) has antimicrobial, molluscicidal, and anti-inflammatory properties. Bark decoction is used for treating diarrhea and dysentery. Leaf extracts have shown anti-inflammatory and analgesic activity, and the plant is also used for treating anthelmintic conditions and skin diseases.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction prepared and taken internally; leaf extract applied externally; cashew nut shell oil used topically for warts and corns.

medicinal_uses: The whole plant is used for treating fever, wounds, and skin conditions. The plant paste is applied on boils and abscesses to draw out infection. The decoction mixed with other herbs is used for treating measles and chicken pox, and the root preparation is used for reducing inflammation and treating joint pain.
dosage_preparation: Plant paste mixed with jaggery for 100 ml decoction taken with 25 ml sesame oil; applied externally with equal parts of plant and turmeric for 7 days for skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: The root decoction is used for treating urinary tract infections and as a demulcent for soothing irritated mucous membranes. It is also used for treating leucorrhoea and menstrual disorders. The plant paste is applied on swellings and inflammatory conditions for relief.
dosage_preparation: 10 grams of root boiled in water, decoction taken with honey and sugar; root paste applied on affected areas; decoction of 3 parts root with 1 part adjuvant taken for 40 days (200 ml).

medicinal_uses: The whole plant is used for treating bronchial asthma and respiratory conditions. It has antiviral activity effective against plant viruses and has shown antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant is also used for treating amenorrhea (absence of menstruation) and as a galactagogue.
dosage_preparation: 20 grams whole plant boiled in 200 ml water, reduced to 50 ml, taken with appropriate adjuvant at bedtime for amenorrhea; plant juice used externally for skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: The rhizome of Costus speciosus contains diosgenin and is used in traditional medicine. It has anti-inflammatory, antifertility, and antispasmodic properties. Studies have shown effects on uterine musculature, and it is used in treatment of various conditions including inflammation and reproductive health issues.
dosage_preparation: Rhizome decoction and extract preparations are used medicinally.

latin_name: Acacia nilotica (L.) Willd. ex Delile ssp. indica (Benth.) Brenan
medicinal_uses: Acacia nilotica bark is astringent and used for diarrhea, dysentery, and wound healing. The gum (Acacia gum) is used as a demulcent and for treating sore throats. The pods and bark decoction is gargled for dental problems and used for skin diseases. It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction for gargling; gum dissolved in water; pod powder applied externally.

medicinal_uses: Xylia xylocarpa bark is used in traditional medicine for treating diarrhea and dysentery. The heartwood yields a dye called xylodin. The bark decoction is used for intestinal disorders and as an astringent. The wood is extremely hard and valued for construction.
dosage_preparation: 50 grams bark boiled in 200 mL water, reduced to 25 mL; taken with honey. Also bark paste applied on wounds.

medicinal_uses: Maesa indica leaves are used in folk medicine for treating skin diseases and wounds. The plant has anthelmintic properties and is used against intestinal parasites. Leaf paste is applied externally for skin conditions. About 3 grams of leaf powder is given with water 3-4 times daily for treating parasitic infections and digestive ailments.
dosage_preparation: 3 grams of leaf powder taken with water, 3-4 times daily for parasitic infections.

medicinal_uses: Tulsi (Holy Basil) is one of the most widely used medicinal plants with extensive pharmacological properties. It is used for treating cough, cold, bronchitis, fever, arthritis, hypertension, and as an adaptogenic (anti-stress) herb. It has demonstrated antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, radio-protective, anticancer, and immunomodulatory activities. The leaf juice is used for reducing blood sugar (fasting and post-prandial), treating asthma, cataracts, and skin diseases.
dosage_preparation: Leaf juice 10-15 mL taken with honey for cough and cold; seed powder with water for urinary disorders; 0.1 g leaf powder for various conditions. Decoction and fresh juice preparations are common.

medicinal_uses: Artemisia pallens (Davana) is primarily used as an aromatic plant. In folk medicine, the plant is used for treating pneumonia and other respiratory tract infections. It has insecticidal properties against Tribolium castaneum. The fresh plant paste is applied on wounds caused by maggot infestations to promote healing. The plant decoction applied on the head helps control lice within 6-7 days.
dosage_preparation: Plant decoction applied externally for lice. Fresh leaf paste applied on maggot-infested wounds.

medicinal_uses: Spermacoce hispida is used in folk medicine for treating gastrointestinal disorders, diarrhea, and dysentery. The leaf and seed paste is applied on chronic skin diseases. The seeds are used as a digestive aid. Fresh leaf juice is taken for diarrhea and dysentery with good results within 10-15 days.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste mixed with buttermilk; seed paste applied on affected areas for skin ailments. Half teaspoon powder mixed with curd taken internally.

medicinal_uses: Cassia alata is widely known as the 'Ringworm plant' due to its strong antifungal activity. The leaf extract is effective against ringworm (dermatophytic infections), eczema, and other skin diseases. It also has anti-inflammatory, analgesic, wound healing, antibacterial, laxative, and hyperglycemic properties. Studies have confirmed its efficacy against various fungal pathogens.
dosage_preparation: Fresh leaf juice applied on ringworm-affected areas. Leaf paste applied externally for skin infections.

medicinal_uses: Jasminum sambac (Arabian Jasmine) flowers are used as a lactifuge (to suppress breast milk secretion). The flowers and leaves have cooling properties. The plant is used in treating eye infections, skin ailments, and ulcers. Flower oil is used in aromatherapy for its calming effects. Root paste is applied externally on wounds and ulcers.
dosage_preparation: Flower garland applied on head to cool; leaf paste applied on skin; flower decoction taken for 5-6 days for ulcers. Leaf juice mixed with oil applied 2-3 months for hair growth.

medicinal_uses: Ruta chalepensis (Common Rue) is used for treating skin conditions including vitiligo (leucoderma). It has antifertility, contraceptive, and abortifacient properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory, antinociceptive, molluscicidal, and antithrombotic effects. It is used topically for phytophotodermatitis treatment. Studies show it repels insects and has antimicrobial activity against various pathogens.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste applied externally. Leaf decoction taken internally for various conditions. Caution advised due to phototoxic properties.

medicinal_uses: Mesua ferrea (Nagakesara/Iron Wood) flowers are astringent and used for bleeding piles, dysentery, and menorrhagia. The stamens yield an essential oil with antimicrobial and anthelmintic properties. The bark is used as an astringent. The seed oil is applied for skin diseases and rheumatism. It has antiasthmatic, anti-inflammatory, CNS depressant, and antifertility activities. The flowers are used in Ayurvedic formulations for bleeding disorders.
dosage_preparation: Flower powder 2-3 grams with honey for bleeding disorders. Seed oil applied externally for skin diseases. Bark decoction used as an astringent.

medicinal_uses: Rauwolfia tetraphylla roots contain reserpine and related alkaloids with antihypertensive properties. The root extract is used for treating hypertension (high blood pressure) and as a sedative. It has antifungal activity against Trichosporium vesiculosum, Macrophomina khaseolina, and Aspergillus flavus. Root paste is applied on wounds caused by insect stings to promote healing.
dosage_preparation: Root paste applied externally on insect stings and wounds. Root preparations used for hypertension under medical supervision.

medicinal_uses: Portulaca quadrifida is used in folk medicine for treating erysipelas (skin inflammation), leucorrhoea, and prickly heat. The plant has cooling properties and is applied as a paste for skin conditions. It also has contraceptive effects as demonstrated in animal studies on reproductive organs. Fresh plant juice is used for burning sensation and urinary problems.
dosage_preparation: Plant paste applied externally for erysipelas and prickly heat. 100 mL fresh juice taken for 7-8 days for skin conditions. 5 grams powder taken with buttermilk for 15-20 days for leucorrhoea.

medicinal_uses: Polygonum glabrum is used for treating stomach infections, fever, and skin diseases. The plant has antibacterial, antitubercular, and antifungal activities. The leaf extract has shown activity against various pathogens. The root decoction is used for digestive disorders and as an astringent.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste applied on affected skin areas 3 times, twice daily for 2 weeks. Root decoction for fevers. Leaf juice applied with oil on the body for 40-80 days for chronic skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: Bacopa monnieri (Brahmi) is one of the most important nootropic (brain tonic) herbs in Ayurveda. It is extensively used for improving memory, learning, and cognitive function, especially in mentally retarded children and elderly with memory loss. It has anti-anxiety, anti-inflammatory, anticonvulsant, and antioxidant properties. Studies show it improves memory and learning in rats. Clinical trials demonstrate efficacy in treating ADHD, schizophrenia, insomnia, irritability, and stress. It is also used as Medhya Rasayana (brain rejuvenator) in Ayurvedic medicine.
dosage_preparation: Brahmi ghrita and Brahmi rasayana are classical Ayurvedic preparations. Fresh juice or powder preparations used for cognitive enhancement. Various branded formulations (Mentat, BR-16A) are available.

medicinal_uses: Plumbago auriculata (Blue Plumbago) root is used in folk medicine for treating warts. The root paste mixed with aromatic oil is applied on warts, and with regular daily application, the warts gradually diminish and fall off. The plant is closely related to Plumbago zeylanica (white chitramula) which has more extensive medicinal uses.
dosage_preparation: Root paste mixed with aromatic oil applied daily on warts until they fall off.

medicinal_uses: Jacaranda mimosifolia bark and leaf extracts have shown phytochemical and antimicrobial activity. The bark extract is used for treating boll weevil infestations. Studies show cytotoxic activity against various cell lines, making it a potential anticancer plant. The bark decoction is also used in folk medicine for sexually transmitted diseases and syphilis.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction taken internally; leaf extract applied externally.

medicinal_uses: Chlorophytum tuberosum tubers are used as a galactagogue (to increase breast milk production) and as a general tonic. The tuber preparations are used for treating leucorrhoea and as a rejuvenating herb. The tuber paste or decoction is used as a nutritive supplement.
dosage_preparation: 50 mL tuber decoction taken for 40 days to improve tissue strength. Tuber powder mixed with milk and sugar taken daily for leucorrhoea (1 spoon powder, 1 spoon sugar with milk).

medicinal_uses: Passiflora edulis (Passion Fruit) has sedative and hypnotic properties. The fruit rind extract is used as a natural sedative and sleep aid. The fruit juice is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, beneficial for overall health. Studies have demonstrated its anxiolytic effects and ability to reduce blood pressure. It also has antifungal activity against keratinophilic fungi.
dosage_preparation: Fruit rind kept for 40 days, then decoction prepared as a sedative. Fruit juice consumed fresh for nutritional benefits.

medicinal_uses: Turnera ulmifolia has oral anti-inflammatory and anti-ulcerogenic activities. The hydroalcoholic extract of the plant has demonstrated significant gastroprotective properties. The leaves and flowers are used for treating gastric ulcers and inflammatory conditions. The plant has expectorant properties and is used for respiratory tract complaints in folk medicine.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste applied externally; leaf decoction taken for inflammation. Doses of 2 spoons taken twice daily for 2 weeks for digestive issues.

medicinal_uses: The bark and leaves are used to treat piles (hemorrhoids) and diarrhea. A decoction of the bark is used as a gargle for gum and teeth ailments. The gum exudate is used for digestive complaints.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction taken 2 times daily for 2 days for diarrhea; leaf paste applied externally for skin conditions. Bark powder 2-8 parts mixed with buttermilk for piles.

medicinal_uses: The fruit is extremely rich in Vitamin C and is used to treat scurvy and general debility. The fruit juice is used for liver disorders and as an astringent. It is also used for diarrhea and dysentery.
dosage_preparation: Fresh fruit juice taken 10-20 drops with water for stomach ailments. Fruit paste applied externally for skin conditions; decoction of 6 fruits taken 2-3 times daily.

medicinal_uses: The bark has antipyretic and diuretic properties and is used as a lung tonic. The leaves contain hispidulin which has anti-inflammatory action. The flowers are dried and smoked as a substitute for tobacco to relieve sinusitis and asthma.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction 100 ml twice daily for fever; leaf extract applied for ringworm infections. Dried flowers smoked for respiratory congestion.

medicinal_uses: The plant is used for treating orchitis (swelling of testicles) and urinary disorders. The leaf poultice is applied externally for boils and abscesses. The root decoction is used as an antidote for alcoholic intoxication.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste applied externally for 1-2 days for abscesses. Root decoction with 2 cups of water boiled to half, for orchitis treatment 5 grams daily. Application continued for 15-20 days.

medicinal_uses: The gum of the tree is used for treating bleeding disorders and diarrhea. The bark decoction is used as an aphrodisiac and to treat impotency. The root bark is used for piles (hemorrhoids) treatment.
dosage_preparation: Gum resin 2 grams with honey taken twice daily for 3 days for diarrhea. Bark decoction 1 cup taken twice daily for 40 days for impotency. Fruit pulp boiled in 200 ml water for piles, taken for 30-60 days.

medicinal_uses: The leaves are used in traditional medicine for treating skin diseases and wounds. The plant contains securinine alkaloid which has CNS stimulant properties. The leaf paste is applied to treat scabies and other skin infections.
dosage_preparation: Leaf paste applied externally for skin conditions. Fresh leaf juice 3 drops instilled in ear for 2 days for earache. Bark decoction used as wash for wounds.

medicinal_uses: The plant has immunological properties and is used traditionally for treating fevers, urinary disorders, and general debility. The root decoction is given for leucorrhoea and as a tonic. The leaves are used as a poultice for swellings and boils.
dosage_preparation: Root decoction with 4 grams powder boiled in water, taken once daily for 40 days for leucorrhoea. Leaf paste applied externally for swellings. Crushed root 2 tablespoons taken 2-3 times daily for urinary problems.

medicinal_uses: The fruit has CNS depressant properties and is used for treating diarrhea and dysentery. The bark and leaf extracts show antimicrobial activity. The ripe fruit juice is used as a cough remedy and for treating constipation.
dosage_preparation: Fruit juice 1 cup taken morning and night for constipation. Bark decoction used as a wash for skin conditions. Fruit pulp 2 tablespoons mixed with sugar taken 2-3 times daily for diarrhea.

medicinal_uses: Garlic is one of the most extensively studied medicinal plants. It has proven hypoglycemic, hypotensive, hypocholesterolemic, antiatherosclerotic, and antithrombotic activities. It is used for treating cardiovascular diseases, hypertension, diabetes, infections, and has strong antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties.
dosage_preparation: Raw garlic cloves 2-3 taken daily for cardiovascular health. Garlic oil drops for ear infections. Garlic paste applied externally for wounds and skin infections.

medicinal_uses: The whole plant is extensively used as a hepatoprotective agent, particularly for jaundice and hepatitis B. It has proven antiviral activity against Hepatitis B virus and shows contraceptive, antifertility, hypoglycemic, and hypotensive effects. It is also used for treating kidney stones, urinary disorders, and diabetes.
dosage_preparation: Whole plant decoction taken twice daily for jaundice. Fresh plant juice 10-20 ml for liver disorders. Plant powder used in formulations for kidney stones and urinary problems.

medicinal_uses: The plant has antifertility properties - leaf extracts show anti-estrogenic activity affecting the oestrous cycle in rats. The leaves also have antifungal and neuropharmacological activities. The essential oil from leaves shows antimicrobial properties against fungi and bacteria.
dosage_preparation: Leaf extract used in traditional preparations. Fresh leaves ground with water for topical application. Specific dosages referenced in research contexts (25-300 mg/kg in animal studies).

medicinal_uses: The fern is used traditionally for treating burns, wounds, cough, and respiratory conditions. Research has confirmed antibacterial, antifungal, and nematicidal activities. The plant is also used in Ayurvedic formulations for blood disorders and as a general tonic.
dosage_preparation: Leaf decoction used for cough and respiratory ailments. Root powder applied externally for wound healing. Plant used in compound Ayurvedic formulations for blood purification.

medicinal_uses: Mango has antihyperglycemic, antiplasmodial, antipyretic, antitumor, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bark extract is used for diarrhea and dysentery, the seed kernel for intestinal worms and diarrhea, and the leaf decoction for diabetes management. Mango kernel oil has antifungal properties.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction for diarrhea and dysentery. Seed kernel powder 2-3 grams with buttermilk for intestinal worms. Leaf decoction for managing blood sugar levels. Vimang (bark extract) used as anti-inflammatory preparation.

medicinal_uses: The plant is used traditionally for treating fevers, headaches, and stomach disorders. The herb has antispasmodic and menstrual regulation properties. It is used externally for treating swellings, joint pains, and ear infections.
dosage_preparation: Fresh juice with honey and pepper for stomach ailments, 3 drops with oil for ear infections. Leaf paste warmed and applied for joint swelling. Plant decoction taken for menstrual disorders.

medicinal_uses: Corn silk (style/stigma) has proven diuretic properties and is used for urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Maize bran helps control blood sugar and cholesterol in diabetic patients. The grain has wound-healing properties and the corn peptide facilitates alcohol metabolism.
dosage_preparation: Corn silk decoction: 20 grams boiled in 100 ml water, taken 50 ml morning and evening for 8-10 days for urinary problems. Corn silk tea for kidney stone prevention. Maize bran incorporated in diet for cholesterol management.

medicinal_uses: The plant has anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, antifertility, and antibacterial properties. The root and flower extracts show significant antiviral activity. The plant is used traditionally for treating rheumatic conditions and as a mosquito repellent.
dosage_preparation: Root paste 50 grams ground with 100 ml water, filtered and taken 50 ml per dose. Flower decoction applied externally. Treatment continued for 3 doses over 3 days, repeated for 1-2 days for fever management.

medicinal_uses: The plant has cytotoxic and antitumor activity due to scopadulcic acid. It shows analgesic, anti-inflammatory, sympathomimetic, and antiviral properties. Traditionally used for treating tumors, diabetes, and as a pain-relieving agent.
dosage_preparation: Leaf extract 4 grams with sugar taken once daily for diabetes. Plant decoction: 10 grams boiled in 100 ml water with 1 gram pepper, filtered and taken 50 ml doses 3 times daily for 1-2 days for fever.

medicinal_uses: The plant root and bark have antibacterial and antimicrobial properties. The latex is used traditionally for treating wounds and skin infections. The root decoction is used for treating inflammatory conditions and as a tonic.
dosage_preparation: Root bark decoction 20 grams boiled in 100 ml water, taken 50 ml morning and evening. Latex applied for 10-15 days for skin conditions. Root paste applied externally 40 minutes before washing for skin diseases.

medicinal_uses: The plant has nematicidal properties against Meloidogyne incognita. The seed and pith are used traditionally for treating ulcers, boils, and wounds. The Ayurvedic preparations from this plant are used for blood disorders, skin diseases, and as a general tonic.
dosage_preparation: Leaf extract used in compound formulations. Seed kernel powder 25 grams mixed with water, taken 50 ml doses with ghee 25 ml for muscle pains. Pith decoction applied externally for boils and skin conditions for 40 days.

medicinal_uses: The latex has purgative and rubefacient properties and is used for treating warts, corns, and skin growths. The stem juice mixed with oil is used for ear infections and as a purgative. The plant has neuropharmacological properties and the latex contains ingenane-type diterpene esters with biological activity.
dosage_preparation: Latex from fresh stem applied directly on warts for 10-20 days for removal. Stem juice 100 grams mixed with castor oil 500 ml and other ingredients, boiled and used 200 ml as purgative. Corn plaster preparations applied with latex for 4 days for corn removal.

medicinal_uses: The bark and flowers are used to treat chronic headaches through shirodhara therapy. The leaf decoction is applied externally for skin diseases and wounds, and the bark decoction is used to relieve inflammation. Research has shown efficacy of shirodhara treatment with this plant's preparations in managing chronic headache over 3-4 weeks.
dosage_preparation: Shirodhara oil preparation applied daily for 25 patients over 30 minutes per session for 21 days showed relief from chronic headaches.

medicinal_uses: In Mexico and other countries, the plant's leaves are used as a traditional anti-epileptic remedy. The aqueous extract of Cestrum nocturnum leaves shows insecticidal activity against cockroaches (Periplaneta americana). The plant also contains saponins with pharmacological activity.
dosage_preparation: Leaf extract used externally; all plant parts used in various traditional preparations.

medicinal_uses: The seed extract (containing maslinic acid and bixin) has demonstrated antimicrobial, antifungal, and antileishmanial properties. Root bark decoction is used as a gargle for sore throat, and the seed paste is applied externally for skin conditions. Seeds are used to treat dysentery, kidney diseases, and as a blood purifier, and the leaf extract shows hyperglycaemic activity.
dosage_preparation: 25 grams of annatto seed powder mixed with 200 ml water, boiled to 50 ml decoction; 5 grams of dried root powder mixed with water for gargle; seed paste applied for 3-4 days for skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: The plant is traditionally used as an anthelmintic (deworming agent). Research has confirmed the antigenotoxicity potential of Quisqualis indica against somatic cell genotoxicity. The fruit and seed are used to expel roundworms and threadworms in children, and the plant shows activity against intestinal parasites.
dosage_preparation: Roasted seeds given to children 3 times a day for 3-4 days to expel intestinal worms. Leaf poultice applied externally for skin conditions.

medicinal_uses: Aloe vera gel is widely used for wound healing, anti-inflammatory effects, and treating burns. The gel has demonstrated immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and wound healing properties in research studies. It is also used for skin protection, treating radiation injuries, and has shown hepatoprotective and antidiabetic activity. The plant is used in traditional medicine for digestive disorders, constipation, and as an ethanol metabolism enhancer.
dosage_preparation: Fresh gel applied topically for burns and wounds; juice taken internally for digestive ailments; also used in Aloe vera-based commercial gels for skin protection.

medicinal_uses: Ginger rhizome is used extensively in Ayurvedic medicine for digestive disorders, nausea, and cough. It has demonstrated anti-ulcer effects, cardiotonicproperties, inhibition of Aspergillus parasiticus growth, and cytotoxic-drug-induced vomiting suppression. The rhizome is also used in Dhanya Panchak Kashay for gastric secretion disorders (hyperchlorhydria and hypochlorhydria), and ginger essential oil contains three new monoacyldigalactosylglycerols with medicinal properties.
dosage_preparation: Fresh ginger juice or dried ginger (sonthi) powder used in various Ayurvedic formulations; commonly used in decoctions (kashaya) for cold, cough and digestive complaints.

medicinal_uses: The bark and leaves are used medicinally. The bark decoction is given for diarrhoea and dysentery. The plant is traditionally used for treating stomach ailments and has astringent properties. Preparations from the bark are used as a tonic and for general debility.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction: 100 ml bark soaked overnight, boiled to 250 ml, reduced to 50 ml; taken 16 ml dose with honey for stomach conditions; bark powder (30 g) mixed with buttermilk taken 10 ml 3 times daily for 1-2 months for diarrhoea.

medicinal_uses: The whole plant is used to relieve cough and fever. It is applied as a poultice for boils and skin eruptions, taken 3 times daily for 3 days. The plant extract is used for cold and fever by mixing 20 grams of the fresh herb with 100 ml water, boiling to reduce to 50 ml, and taking with jaggery at bedtime for 8-10 days. Research has confirmed its pharmacognostical and phytochemical properties.
dosage_preparation: Leaf and stem decoction: 20 grams boiled in 100 ml water reduced to 50 ml, taken with jaggery at bedtime for 8-10 days for respiratory ailments.

medicinal_uses: Shikakai pods are traditionally used for hair care and as a natural shampoo. The saponin-rich pods have spasmolytic, spermicidal, and molluscicidal activity. The pod paste is used externally for treating dandruff and promoting hair growth. An Ayurvedic hair care formulation combining Acacia sinuata with other herbs like Albizea amara has been documented for polyherbal hair care preparations.
dosage_preparation: Pods soaked in water and used as hair wash; pod powder 500 grams mixed with other herbs (Albizea amara 100g, tulsi 100g, neem 50g, etc.) for polyherbal hair care formulation.

medicinal_uses: The bark and fruit pulp are used medicinally. The bark decoction mixed with 10 grams of jaggery and honey is taken in 100 ml doses for treating dysentery and diarrhoea. The fruit pulp is used for cooling and is rich in nutrients including proteins (19.9%), carbohydrates, calcium (337 mg/100g), and iron (109 mg/100g). The bark is also used for gum inflammation and tooth problems.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction: 10 grams bark boiled with honey in 100 ml water, reduced to 50 ml; taken with jaggery for 5-10 days. Fruit pulp (5-10 grams) taken for digestive complaints.

medicinal_uses: Champaca flowers and bark possess antifertility activity and have been shown to induce resorption of implants and prevent pregnancy in animal studies. The flower oil is used in perfumery and aromatherapy. The bark decoction is used for treating fever, and the root bark is used as an emmenagogue. The plant's essential oils have antibacterial and antifungal properties.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction used for fever; flowers used in oil preparations; root bark extract taken for 2-3 weeks for menstrual regulation. Flower oil (Etaerio of Follicles) used for respiratory conditions.

medicinal_uses: The plant's fruits are used in traditional medicine for cough, liver disorders, and as a digestive. The root decoction is used as an anthelmintic and for treating toothache. Solanum torvum belongs to the same family as S. indicum and contains steroidal saponins with antifertility properties. The unripe fruits are eaten as a vegetable and have diuretic properties.
dosage_preparation: Fruit and root decoctions used in traditional preparations; unripe fruits consumed as vegetable for nutritional and medicinal benefits.

medicinal_uses: The rhizome is used to treat jaundice by grinding it with fresh Hanumana hastha rhizome and taking 5-10 grams of the juice with buttermilk for 20 ml dose, once daily for 3 sessions over 4 days. It is also used for treating fractures and bone disorders by applying the rhizome paste externally for 3-4 days. For memory enhancement, the rhizome decoction (10 grams boiled in 300 ml water) is used with pepper and cumin.
dosage_preparation: For jaundice: 5-10 grams rhizome juice with buttermilk for 4 days. For fractures: rhizome paste applied for 3-4 days with 5-10 grams taken internally. For memory: 10 grams rhizome in 300 ml decoction.

medicinal_uses: Castor oil (from seeds) is a well-known purgative and is used for constipation, rheumatic pain, and skin conditions. The leaves have anti-inflammatory activity and are applied as a poultice for swellings and inflammations. The plant has hepatoprotective activity, larvicidal properties, and the oil shows antifungal effects. Research has confirmed the anti-inflammatory activity of Ricinus communis (Eranda) and its hepatoprotective properties for liver disorders.
dosage_preparation: Castor oil: 5-10 ml taken orally as purgative. Leaf poultice applied externally for joint pain and swellings. Root bark decoction used for rheumatic conditions.

medicinal_uses: The whole plant is used to treat dysentery, diarrhoea, and skin diseases. The latex is applied topically for warts and skin eruptions. The plant decoction mixed with sambar (20 grams in 100 ml) reduced to 50 ml is taken for bowel disorders. It also has antidermatophytic activity and is used for treating ringworm and other fungal skin infections.
dosage_preparation: Whole plant decoction: 20 grams boiled in 100 ml water, reduced to 50 ml, taken with jaggery. For skin conditions: latex applied externally for 8-10 days.

medicinal_uses: The essential oil of Chenopodium ambrosioides has antidermatophytic and antifungal properties and is used against ringworm. The plant extract shows pharmacological activity including anthelmintic effects. The whole plant is used in traditional medicine for treating intestinal worms and skin fungal infections. Research has confirmed its antidermatophytic action against fungal pathogens.
dosage_preparation: Leaf and stem extract used externally for fungal skin infections. Essential oil applied topically for ringworm treatment. Research doses: 100-200 mg/gram concentrations for antifungal activity.

medicinal_uses: Holatulasi (Ocimum americanum/O. canum) essential oils show strong antifungal and antimicrobial activity against dermatophytes and pathogenic fungi. The seeds have been used for improving glucose tolerance in diabetics through long-term dietary supplementation. The plant's essential oils serve as natural antifungal agents against plant pathogens including Pythium aphanidermatum, and also show insecticidal properties against stored grain pests.
dosage_preparation: Essential oil preparations used for antifungal applications. Seeds used as dietary supplement for diabetic management. Leaf decoction for fever and digestive complaints.

medicinal_uses: The tree, also known as Pride of India (Jarul), is used in traditional medicine. The bark and leaves are used for treating diarrhoea and dysentery due to their astringent properties. The plant is related to Lagerstroemia speciosa and L. flos-reginae, which are known for their antidiabetic properties. The flowers are ornamental and the bark is used in tanning.
dosage_preparation: Bark decoction used for diarrhoea and dysentery; leaf preparations used as an astringent.
